Sales & Commercial Performance Intelligence
CUSTOMER & COMMERCIAL EXCELLENCE

WHAT THE AGENT DOES

Provides the CEO and President with real-time sales performance intelligence — pipeline coverage, conversion rates, deal velocity, win/loss analysis, and sales team productivity — going beyond the headline revenue number to the leading indicators that predict whether the company will hit its commercial targets.
Revenue is a lagging indicator — by the time it misses, it is too late to act. This agent gives the CEO the leading indicators that predict revenue 90 days in advance.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

Layer 1 — Pipeline Coverage Analysis: Analyses sales pipeline coverage ratio by BU, product, and geography — at 3× pipeline coverage, revenue targets are typically achievable; below 2× coverage, miss probability is high. Updated weekly.
Layer 2 — Deal Velocity Monitoring: Tracks average time to close by deal size and segment. Identifies when deals are stalling in specific pipeline stages — and whether the stall is a product, pricing, competitive, or sales process issue.
Layer 3 — Win/Loss Analysis: Analyses closed deals to identify systematic win and loss patterns — which segments is the company winning, against which competitors, at what price points, with what product capabilities? Where is the company losing and why?
Layer 4 — Sales Team Productivity: Tracks individual and team-level sales productivity — quota attainment distribution, activity-to-outcome ratios, ramp time for new sellers, and manager coaching effectiveness.
Layer 5 — Revenue Forecast Accuracy: Tracks the accuracy of sales forecasts — is the sales team calling revenue close to actual, or is there systematic optimism bias? Forecast accuracy is itself a leading indicator of pipeline data quality and management discipline.

Strategic Partnership & Ecosystem Intelligence
CUSTOMER & COMMERCIAL EXCELLENCE

WHAT THE AGENT DOES

Manages the organisation's strategic partnership portfolio — identifying, developing, and measuring the value of partnerships that extend market reach, add capability, or provide competitive differentiation — ensuring the CEO and President invest partnership resources where they generate measurable commercial and strategic return.
Most companies have too many partnerships and too little value from any of them — this agent identifies which partnerships to invest in, which to exit, and where new partnerships could unlock disproportionate growth.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

Layer 1 — Partnership Portfolio Assessment: Maps all existing partnerships — commercial, technology, distribution, and strategic — against the value they generate: revenue, leads, capability access, or strategic positioning. Many partnerships exist by inertia, not value.
Layer 2 — Partner Performance Measurement: Tracks the performance of each active partnership — joint pipeline generated, revenue contributed, co-marketing effectiveness, and executive relationship health. Separates high-performing from underperforming partnerships.
Layer 3 — Partnership Opportunity Identification: Scans the ecosystem for new partnership opportunities — companies whose capabilities, customer access, or distribution could create disproportionate value for the company if combined through a formal partnership.
Layer 4 — Partnership Negotiation Intelligence: Provides intelligence for partnership negotiations — comparable deal terms in the market, partner leverage points, exclusivity considerations, and financial model for the value split.
Layer 5 — Ecosystem Strategy: Develops a coherent ecosystem strategy — which partnerships are strategic (invest and deepen), which are transactional (maintain cost-efficiently), and which should be terminated to free up relationship and resource bandwidth.

Go-to-Market Effectiveness & Brand Intelligence
CUSTOMER & COMMERCIAL EXCELLENCE

WHAT THE AGENT DOES

Provides the CEO and President with intelligence on the effectiveness of the company's go-to-market investment — marketing ROI, brand awareness and sentiment, demand generation effectiveness, and channel performance — ensuring that marketing spend is driving measurable commercial outcomes, not just activity.
Marketing spend without attribution is a black hole in the P&L. This agent transforms the conversation from 'what did marketing spend?' to 'what commercial outcome did marketing produce?'

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

Layer 1 — Marketing ROI Attribution: Attributes revenue and pipeline to specific marketing programmes — events, digital, content, PR, and account-based marketing. Computes cost-per-opportunity and cost-per-closed-deal by programme and channel.
Layer 2 — Brand Awareness and Sentiment: Tracks brand awareness in target segments, brand perception metrics, share of voice vs. competitors, and brand sentiment across all touchpoints. Quantifies the brand premium the company commands in its markets.
Layer 3 — Demand Generation Funnel: Tracks the full demand generation funnel — from awareness to interest to consideration to intent — identifying where the funnel is leaking and where investment would generate the highest incremental conversion.
Layer 4 — Content and Thought Leadership Effectiveness: Measures the effectiveness of content and thought leadership investment — CEO publications, whitepapers, speaking engagements — in generating awareness, consideration, and commercial opportunity.
Layer 5 — Channel Performance: Benchmarks each marketing channel — digital, events, partner, PR, analyst relations — against both spend efficiency and contribution to strategic positioning goals, not just demand generation.

II  Innovation & Industry Positioning
Technology Strategy, Thought Leadership, Competitive Intelligence, Innovation Culture, Digital Transformation & CEO Intelligence


31
Technology & AI Strategy Intelligence
INNOVATION & INDUSTRY POSITIONING

WHAT THE AGENT DOES

Provides the CEO and board with intelligence on the technology and AI landscape — emerging technologies that could disrupt or differentiate the business, the competitive implications of AI adoption, and the investment decisions required to build or maintain technology advantage — ensuring the CEO is leading the organisation's technology future rather than being disrupted by it.
AI will not put your company out of business — a competitor who uses AI better than you will. This agent ensures the CEO is making technology strategy decisions with intelligence, not anxiety.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

Layer 1 — Technology Landscape Monitoring: Monitors the global technology landscape for developments relevant to the company's business model — AI capability advances, platform shifts, infrastructure changes, and open-source developments that could be adopted rapidly by competitors.
Layer 2 — Competitive Technology Assessment: Assesses the technology capabilities of the company's top-5 competitors — what AI and automation capabilities are they building? What is the competitive advantage or disadvantage this creates for the company?
Layer 3 — Internal AI Readiness: Assesses the company's internal readiness for AI adoption — data quality and governance, infrastructure maturity, talent availability, and cultural readiness for AI-assisted decision-making.
Layer 4 — Technology Investment Prioritisation: Evaluates technology investment proposals against a framework — strategic importance, competitive necessity, implementation risk, and financial return. Recommends a technology investment portfolio for CEO and board approval.
Layer 5 — AI Governance: Develops the AI governance framework for the company — responsible AI principles, bias monitoring, explainability standards, and regulatory compliance — ensuring AI adoption enhances rather than creates new enterprise risks.

32
Industry Leadership & Thought Leadership Positioning
INNOVATION & INDUSTRY POSITIONING

WHAT THE AGENT DOES

Manages the CEO's and company's industry positioning — thought leadership content, industry association engagement, regulatory participation, awards and recognition, and speaking engagements — systematically building the reputation assets that translate into commercial advantage, talent attraction, and pricing power.
Industry leadership is not vanity — it is a commercial strategy. Companies whose CEOs are visible, credible thought leaders attract better talent, win more deals, and command higher prices.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

Layer 1 — CEO Thought Leadership Strategy: Develops the CEO's annual thought leadership agenda — the 3–5 topics where the CEO has genuine insight and where visibility would create commercial and strategic value. Every CEO activity must map to a strategic objective.
Layer 2 — Content Intelligence: Monitors which topics are gaining traction in the industry — search volume, conference agendas, journalist enquiries, analyst focus areas — and aligns the CEO's content calendar to the conversations that matter.
Layer 3 — Speaking and Conference Strategy: Identifies the speaking opportunities with the highest strategic value — conferences attended by target customers, investors, talent, and regulators. Prioritises quality over quantity.
Layer 4 — Industry Association Engagement: Identifies the industry associations, advisory boards, and government consultation processes where the company's participation would create influence over regulations and standards that affect the business.
Layer 5 — Recognition and Award Programme: Manages a systematic programme to win industry awards, analyst recognition (Gartner, Forrester), and business press recognition — each award is a proof point in the sales process and talent attraction.

33
Competitive Strategy & War-Gaming Intelligence
INNOVATION & INDUSTRY POSITIONING

WHAT THE AGENT DOES

Provides the CEO with continuous competitive intelligence and structured war-gaming capability — simulating competitor moves, assessing strategic vulnerabilities, and testing the robustness of the company's strategy against the most plausible competitive scenarios — so that the CEO is never surprised by a competitor move.
The best military commanders study their enemies as carefully as they study their own armies. This agent makes competitive intelligence a real-time strategic capability, not an annual conference room exercise.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

Layer 1 — Competitor Deep Profiles: Maintains continuously updated profiles of the top-5 competitors — strategy, financial health, product roadmap signals (from job postings, patents, product updates), key executive moves, and investor backing.
Layer 2 — Move Detection: Detects when competitors are making strategic moves before they are publicly announced — talent hiring patterns that signal R&D direction, geographic expansion signals, and M&A target analysis.
Layer 3 — War-Gaming Scenarios: For the most material competitive threats, runs structured war-gaming exercises — how would the company respond if a competitor launches product X? Enters geography Y? Acquires company Z?
Layer 4 — Defensive and Offensive Response Design: For each plausible competitor move, designs pre-planned company responses — defensive (protect position) and offensive (exploit competitor distraction or vulnerability). CEO has a response plan before the threat materialises.
Layer 5 — Competitive Advantage Sustainability: Assesses the long-term sustainability of the company's competitive advantages — which advantages are durable (hard to replicate), which are temporary (can be copied within 18 months), and where new advantages need to be built.

34
Innovation Culture & Corporate Entrepreneurship
INNOVATION & INDUSTRY POSITIONING

WHAT THE AGENT DOES

Manages the organisation's innovation culture — creating the conditions, processes, and incentives for the company to continuously innovate from within — ensuring that entrepreneurial thinking and risk-taking are not suppressed by the management systems designed to run the current business.
The biggest threat to most established companies is not external competition — it is their own management systems optimised to run the present that systematically kill the future.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

Layer 1 — Innovation Culture Measurement: Measures the organisation's innovation culture health — do employees believe innovation is rewarded? Are they given time and resources to innovate? Is failure treated as learning or career risk? Are ideas reaching decision-makers?
Layer 2 — Internal Venture and Incubation: Manages the internal incubation programme — time-boxed, resourced projects given the freedom to test genuinely new ideas outside the standard governance processes. Tracks conversion from concept to commercial reality.
Layer 3 — Intrapreneurship Recognition: Identifies and celebrates internal innovators — employees whose ideas have created measurable value. Recognition programmes that make innovation a career accelerator, not a career risk.
Layer 4 — External Innovation Ecosystem: Manages the company's engagement with external innovation ecosystems — startup partnerships, hackathons, university research collaborations, and open innovation programmes that bring external thinking into the company.
Layer 5 — Innovation Governance: Ensures that innovation governance is enablement-focused, not compliance-focused — stage gates that provide resources and direction, not bureaucratic barriers. Tracks innovation investment ROI at portfolio level.

35
Digital Transformation Progress & ROI Intelligence
INNOVATION & INDUSTRY POSITIONING

WHAT THE AGENT DOES

Provides the CEO and board with an honest, evidence-based view of the organisation's digital transformation progress — measuring not transformation activity but business outcomes achieved, ensuring that digital investments are producing the revenue growth, cost reduction, and capability improvement they were approved to deliver.
Digital transformation is the most over-invested and under-measured strategic programme in corporate history. This agent measures what actually happened — not what was promised.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

Layer 1 — Digital Investment Tracking: Tracks all digital transformation investments — technology platforms, process automation, AI implementations, and digital skills — against their approved business cases. The commitment made when money was approved is the measurement standard.
Layer 2 — Business Outcome Measurement: Measures the business outcomes of digital programmes — not IT metrics (uptime, implementation on time) but business metrics (revenue growth, cost reduction, process efficiency, customer satisfaction improvement).
Layer 3 — Adoption Rate Monitoring: Tracks technology adoption rates across the organisation — the most common failure point in digital transformation is low adoption of implemented technology. Identifies adoption barriers before investment is wasted.
Layer 4 — Digital Capability Maturity: Assesses the organisation's digital capability maturity — data quality, analytics capability, automation penetration, and AI readiness — against a maturity framework that benchmarks against sector best-in-class.
Layer 5 — Programme Course Correction: For programmes not delivering promised outcomes, provides a structured course correction framework — is the problem technology, adoption, process redesign, or business case assumption quality? Recommendation depends on diagnosis.

36
Long-Range Enterprise Intelligence & CEO Decision Support
INNOVATION & INDUSTRY POSITIONING

WHAT THE AGENT DOES

Provides the CEO with a comprehensive, integrated intelligence capability — synthesising signals from strategy, finance, operations, talent, risk, market, and technology into a single decision-support system that enables the CEO to govern the organisation with the situational awareness that the complexity of the role demands.
The CEO role is the most information-intensive, time-constrained decision-making role in any organisation. This agent is the CEO's intelligence staff — synthesising everything that matters into what requires action today.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

Layer 1 — Enterprise Intelligence Synthesis: Integrates all signals from across the organisation — financial performance, strategic initiative progress, talent health, risk register, competitive landscape, and market dynamics — into a single unified CEO intelligence view.
Layer 2 — Decision Queue Management: Identifies and prioritises all decisions currently requiring CEO input — categorised by urgency, importance, reversibility, and delegation potential. Manages the CEO's decision bandwidth as a scarce strategic resource.
Layer 3 — Signal vs. Noise Filtering: Filters the enormous volume of information reaching the CEO — identifying the 20% of signals that require CEO-level attention and enabling the CEO to delegate or ignore the remaining 80% with confidence.
Layer 4 — Pattern Recognition and Foresight: Identifies non-obvious patterns across the enterprise — where are multiple weak signals converging into a material future event? What emerging issues would a human analyst miss in the noise of day-to-day management?
Layer 5 — CEO Decision Support: For the highest-stakes decisions — strategy, capital allocation, leadership, M&A — provides a structured decision framework: alternatives, evidence for each, risks, and second-order consequences. Not the decision — the framework that makes the decision better.

01
Job Requisition & Headcount Approval
TALENT ACQUISITION


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Manages the end-to-end headcount approval process — from business unit request through budget validation, grade alignment, and CHRO sign-off — before any recruitment begins.
  Ensures every open role is properly justified, budgeted, and approved before resources are committed to recruitment.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Requisition Intake: Reads the manager's headcount request — role title, level, department, reporting line, start date, and business justification. Validates that the role exists in the approved organisation chart.

  Layer 2 — Budget Validation: Cross-checks the requested role against the approved headcount budget for the cost centre and the current financial year. Flags requests that exceed approved headcount.

  Layer 3 — Grade & Compensation Range: Determines the appropriate grade band for the role based on job family, level, and geography. Pulls the approved salary range for budget provisioning.

  Layer 4 — Approval Routing: Routes the requisition through the correct approval chain — line manager → BU head → Finance (budget confirmation) → CHRO. Applies escalation logic if any approver is non-responsive within 48 hours.

  Layer 5 — JD Generation: Once approved, generates a structured job description from the role profile template — responsibilities, required qualifications, preferred skills, and reporting structure. Posts to internal and external job boards.

Grade band and salary range for the role identified
04

Template-based JD populated and reviewed

02
Candidate Sourcing & CV Screening
TALENT ACQUISITION


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Sources candidates from multiple channels — job boards, LinkedIn, employee referrals, and talent pools — screens CVs against the job scorecard, and ranks the pipeline for recruiter review.
  Reduces recruiter time on manual CV screening by 80% while improving quality of shortlist through consistent, objective scoring.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Multi-Channel Sourcing: Monitors job boards (Naukri, LinkedIn, Indeed), campus portals, employee referral submissions, and the internal talent pool for the approved requisition. Aggregates all applications in one pipeline.

  Layer 2 — CV Parsing: Extracts structured data from CVs — education, companies, roles, durations, skills, certifications, and achievements. Handles varied formats (PDF, Word, scanned) using OCR + NLP.

  Layer 3 — Scorecard Matching: Scores each candidate against the job scorecard — mandatory qualifications (pass/fail), experience match (0-40 pts), skills match (0-30 pts), and achievement signals (0-30 pts).

  Layer 4 — Duplicate & Internal Candidate Check: Detects duplicate applications across channels. Checks if applicant is a current employee or recently rejected candidate (cooling-off period).

  Layer 5 — Ranked Shortlist: Delivers a ranked shortlist to the recruiter with score breakdown, profile summary, and source channel. Flags candidates with referral bonus eligibility.

03
Interview Scheduling & Coordination
TALENT ACQUISITION


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Orchestrates the full interview process — scheduling rounds, coordinating panel calendars, sending briefs, collecting feedback, and advancing candidates — without recruiter manual coordination.
  Eliminates the back-and-forth scheduling overhead that typically delays hiring by 5-10 days.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Panel Identification: Reads the interview process template for the role level — number of rounds, interviewers required per round (technical, functional, HRBP, leadership), and format (video/in-person).

  Layer 2 — Calendar Integration: Reads interviewer calendars via calendar API. Identifies common availability windows across all required interviewers that also align with candidate's stated availability.

  Layer 3 — Scheduling Optimisation: Proposes optimal interview slots — minimising time between rounds, avoiding back-to-back on same day, and respecting interviewer load limits (max 2 interviews/day).

  Layer 4 — Pre-Interview Brief: Sends each interviewer a personalised brief — candidate profile, assigned evaluation dimensions, question bank for their dimension, and scoring rubric.

  Layer 5 — Feedback Collection: Sends structured feedback form to each interviewer immediately post-interview. Chases non-respondents at 4-hour and 24-hour intervals. Aggregates feedback across rounds.

04
Background Verification & Pre-Employment Checks
TALENT ACQUISITION


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Initiates, tracks, and adjudicates background verification for all selected candidates — employment history, education, criminal record, reference checks, and CIBIL — ensuring all checks complete before Day 1.
  Eliminates manual follow-up with BGV agencies and prevents compliance gaps from incomplete verifications.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — BGV Scope Determination: Determines the BGV scope for the role — based on level (senior roles get deeper checks), function (Finance/IT get CIBIL), and any regulatory requirements (SEBI-registered entities require SEBI debarment check).

  Layer 2 — Consent & Document Collection: Collects candidate consent (DPDP compliant) and required documents — employment proof for last 3 companies, degree certificates, ID proof, and address proof. Validates completeness.

  Layer 3 — Agency Submission: Submits verified documents to the empanelled BGV agency via their API. Monitors submission acknowledgement and tracks each check category independently.

  Layer 4 — Discrepancy Detection: As reports come in, reads each check result. Classifies discrepancies — Minor (e.g., employment gap < 3 months), Moderate (designation mismatch), Major (employment fraud, criminal record), Critical (degree fabrication).

  Layer 5 — Adjudication & Offer Decision: For clean checks, confirms offer. For discrepancies, routes to HRBP with classification and recommendation. Tracks joining confirmation against BGV completion.

05
Offer Management & Candidate Experience
TALENT ACQUISITION


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Generates, routes, and tracks offer letters — computing the exact compensation package, managing approvals, and handling candidate queries — while maintaining a positive candidate experience throughout.
  Reduces offer-to-acceptance time and prevents offer rejections through proactive engagement.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Compensation Modelling: Reads candidate's current CTC (from BGV or declaration), the approved grade band, budget approved for the role, and the hiring manager's recommendation. Computes the offer within band.

  Layer 2 — Offer Letter Generation: Populates the approved offer letter template — CTC break-up (fixed, variable, retiral benefits, joining bonus if any), reporting structure, role, location, and joining date.

  Layer 3 — Approval Routing: Routes the offer for approval — hiring manager → HRBP → Finance (for above-band or joining bonus) → CHRO (for senior roles). Tracks approvals with timestamps.

  Layer 4 — Delivery & Acceptance Tracking: Delivers the offer letter digitally. Tracks candidate activity — opened, read, signed, or expired. Sends reminders at 48 hours and 5 days if no action.

  Layer 5 — Decline Analysis: If offer is declined, conducts an automated exit interview. Captures reason — compensation, role, location, counter-offer. Flags competitive insights to HRBP.

Compensation Agent

06
Onboarding Automation
EMPLOYEE LIFECYCLE


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Automates the complete new employee onboarding journey from Day -30 (pre-joining) through Day 90 — covering documentation, IT provisioning, policy acknowledgements, induction scheduling, and milestone check-ins.
  Ensures every new joiner reaches productivity faster with a structured, consistent, and engaging first experience.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Pre-Joining Workflow (Day -30 to 0): Sends welcome email, pre-joining document checklist (PAN, Aadhaar, bank details, relieving letter, qualifications), and portal access for form completion. Validates submissions.

  Layer 2 — IT & Infra Provisioning: Triggers IT requests — laptop, email ID, system accesses, software licences, access cards — based on role and department. Monitors provisioning status; flags delays.

  Layer 3 — Induction Scheduling: Books the new joiner into mandatory induction sessions — company overview, HR policies, IT security, compliance, and function-specific orientation. Assigns a buddy from Day 1.

  Layer 4 — Policy Acknowledgement Tracking: Sends all mandatory policy documents (Code of Conduct, POSH, Data Privacy, IT usage). Tracks e-signature completion. Escalates unsigned policies at Day 7 and Day 14.

  Layer 5 — Milestone Check-ins: Sends structured check-in surveys at Day 7, Day 30, and Day 90. Analyses responses for early attrition signals. Triggers HRBP intervention if satisfaction drops below threshold.

07
Employee Data & HRMS Management
EMPLOYEE LIFECYCLE


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Maintains 100% accuracy of employee master data across the HRMS — managing all lifecycle events (promotions, transfers, grade changes, cost centre moves) and ensuring downstream system synchronisation.
  Prevents payroll errors, compliance failures, and reporting inaccuracies that stem from stale or incorrect employee data.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Lifecycle Event Capture: Reads all HR lifecycle events from approvals — promotions, lateral transfers, location changes, reporting line changes, grade revisions, and separation notices.

  Layer 2 — HRMS Update & Validation: Updates the HRMS in real time for each approved event. Validates that the change is internally consistent — grade must match job family, reporting manager must be active, cost centre must be valid.

  Layer 3 — Downstream Synchronisation: Propagates HRMS changes to dependent systems — payroll (for compensation changes), IT (for access rights based on new role), Finance (for cost centre reallocations), and identity management.

  Layer 4 — Data Quality Monitoring: Runs daily data quality checks — missing fields, expired documents (visa, work permits), inactive manager assignments, duplicate records, and phantom employees (on rolls but no activity).

  Layer 5 — Audit Trail: Maintains a complete, immutable audit trail of every HRMS change — who changed what, when, and based on which approval. Required for statutory compliance and internal audit.

08
Separation & Offboarding
EMPLOYEE LIFECYCLE


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Manages the complete employee separation process — resignation, retirement, or termination — including notice period management, exit interviews, asset recovery, full and final settlement computation, and system deprovisioning.
  Ensures clean separation for every employee, protecting the company from legal risk and data security exposure.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Separation Trigger Classification: Reads the separation notice — voluntary (resignation), involuntary (termination), retirement, or end of contract. Classifies and applies the appropriate process and notice period.

  Layer 2 — Notice Period Management: Validates notice period per contract and company policy. Computes notice pay recovery (if early exit) or pay in lieu (if served waived). Manages notice period waiver requests.

  Layer 3 — Exit Interview & Knowledge Capture: Sends structured exit interview to departing employee. Schedules HRBP exit conversation for senior employees. Captures reasons for attrition and key knowledge transfer requirements.

  Layer 4 — Asset & Access Recovery: Generates an asset recovery checklist — laptop, phone, access card, company documents. Triggers IT deprovisioning for all accesses on the last working day. Tracks recovery status.

  Layer 5 — Full & Final Settlement: Computes F&F — pending salary, leave encashment (earned leave balance × daily rate), notice period adjustment, deductions (pending advances, loan recovery), and gratuity (if eligible).

Leave & Attendance Management
EMPLOYEE LIFECYCLE


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Automates leave application processing, balance computation, attendance regularisation, and compliance with leave policy — including policy rules for carry-forward, encashment, and statutory leave entitlements.
  Eliminates manual leave management errors and provides real-time leave position to managers, Finance, and payroll.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Leave Application Processing: Reads leave requests from the HR portal or mobile app. Validates leave type, balance availability, minimum notice period (for planned leave), and manager delegation coverage.

  Layer 2 — Policy Rule Engine: Applies complex leave policy rules — sandwich leave (weekends counted between two leaves), minimum balance requirements, maximum continuous leave limits, and blackout dates.

  Layer 3 — Manager Approval: Routes leave request to line manager with team calendar view (who else is on leave). Applies auto-approval for short leaves (1-2 days) if balance is available and no team conflict.

  Layer 4 — Attendance Reconciliation: Reads biometric / HRMS attendance data. Identifies discrepancies — present without leave (auto-approved), absent without leave (LOP risk), and late logins. Sends regularisation requests.

  Layer 5 — Year-End Processing: Computes year-end leave balance — applies carry-forward rules (max N days), encashment eligibility, and lapse of non-encashable leaves. Updates HRMS and triggers payroll for encashment.

Internal Mobility & Transfers
EMPLOYEE LIFECYCLE


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Manages internal job postings, employee applications, and approved transfers — ensuring internal talent is considered before external hiring, and all transfers are executed with full HRMS and payroll updates.
  Reduces attrition by creating visible career paths and fills roles faster with pre-verified internal talent.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Internal Job Posting: For every approved requisition, posts the role on the internal job board for 5 business days before opening to external candidates. Ensures all eligible employees can apply.

  Layer 2 — Internal Candidate Eligibility: Checks applicant eligibility — minimum tenure in current role (typically 18 months), no PIP or disciplinary action, and manager confirmation that business continuity is manageable.

  Layer 3 — Transfer Process: For approved internal moves, manages the transition — handover plan, notice period within company (typically 30-60 days), and new role start date. Coordinates between losing and gaining managers.

  Layer 4 — Compensation Adjustment: Computes any compensation adjustment on transfer — promotion-linked increase, lateral transfer (usually no change), or skills-premium for critical role. Routes for approval.

  Layer 5 — HRMS Execution: Updates all HRMS fields — department, reporting manager, cost centre, grade, location, and compensation. Triggers IT access changes, payroll updates, and new manager onboarding checklist.

11
Goal Setting & OKR Cascading
PERFORMANCE & LEARNING


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Automates the annual and quarterly goal-setting process — cascading company OKRs to department and individual level, validating SMART goal quality, and ensuring 100% goal coverage across all employees.
  Eliminates goal-setting procrastination and misalignment by making the process structured, time-bound, and visible.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Company OKR Input: Reads the board-approved company-level OKRs for the year — objectives and key results. Maps each objective to the responsible department and function.

  Layer 2 — Department Goal Cascading: For each department, prompts the department head to define department-level OKRs that contribute to at least one company OKR. Validates linkage.

  Layer 3 — Individual Goal Setting: Opens the goal-setting portal for each employee. Guides them to set goals linked to their department OKRs. Validates SMART criteria — Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound.

  Layer 4 — Manager Review & Approval: Routes individual goals to line manager for review and approval. Flags goals that lack measurable KPIs or are misaligned with department priorities.

  Layer 5 — Completion Tracking: Monitors goal-setting completion rates by department. Sends escalating reminders. Reports completion status to CHRO with names of non-compliant managers.

Continuous Performance Feedback
PERFORMANCE & LEARNING


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Enables and tracks continuous 360-degree feedback — prompting check-ins, collecting structured peer and manager feedback, identifying high performers and underperformers, and alerting HRBP to flight risks.
  Replaces the 'surprise' annual review with real-time performance visibility for managers and employees.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Check-in Prompts: Sends monthly manager-employee check-in prompts. Tracks whether 1:1s are happening. Flags managers whose direct reports haven't had a check-in in 30+ days.

  Layer 2 — 360 Feedback Collection: Triggers quarterly 360 feedback cycles. Identifies feedback respondents (peers, cross-functional partners, direct reports). Sends structured feedback forms with competency-based rating scales.

  Layer 3 — Sentiment Analysis: Applies NLP to qualitative feedback comments. Identifies positive patterns (emerging leader, great collaborator), neutral, and concerning patterns (missed deadlines, poor communication).

  Layer 4 — High Performer Flagging: Identifies employees receiving consistently high feedback scores across multiple dimensions over two+ quarters. Flags to HRBP for succession planning and retention action.

  Layer 5 — Flight Risk & PIP Triggers: Identifies early warning signals — declining feedback scores, manager check-in avoidance, or negative sentiment patterns. Triggers HRBP alert for proactive intervention.

13
Annual Performance Review & Ratings
PERFORMANCE & LEARNING


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Manages the annual performance review cycle — self-appraisal, manager rating, normalisation / calibration, final rating assignment, and communication — with transparency and audit trail.
  Ensures performance ratings are fair, calibrated, and consistent across departments.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Self-Appraisal Initiation: Opens self-appraisal forms for all employees. Populates with their approved goals, year-long feedback summary, and learning completions. Sets completion deadline with escalation.

  Layer 2 — Manager Assessment: After self-appraisal, routes to manager for rating against each goal and competency. Pre-fills year-long feedback data to support the manager's assessment.

  Layer 3 — Calibration Support: Compiles department-level rating distribution. Flags statistical anomalies — leniency bias (all Exceeds Expectations), severity bias (all Below Expectations), or central tendency. Prepares calibration data pack.

  Layer 4 — Normalisation: Applies the approved bell curve / forced ranking within departments. Flags employees where proposed rating deviates significantly from bell curve — requires additional justification.

  Layer 5 — Rating Finalisation & Communication: After calibration approval, locks ratings. Generates employee performance summary with final rating, key achievements, and development areas. Routes for employee acknowledgement.

Learning & Development Management
PERFORMANCE & LEARNING


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Identifies learning needs, curates personalised learning paths, manages course completions, tracks mandatory training compliance, and measures learning effectiveness for every employee.
  Connects learning to performance gaps and career aspirations, making L&D investment measurable.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Learning Need Identification: Reads performance review outcomes, 360 feedback themes, and career aspiration data. Maps gaps to available learning content — internal, external, and curated online courses.

  Layer 2 — Personalised Learning Path: Generates a personalised learning plan per employee — prioritised by role criticality, career goal alignment, and manager-identified gaps. Suggests format (self-paced, instructor-led, mentoring).

  Layer 3 — Mandatory Training Compliance: Tracks all mandatory training completions — POSH (annual), Information Security (annual), Anti-Bribery (annual), Fire Safety, and role-specific compliance training. Sends escalating reminders.

  Layer 4 — Learning Engagement Monitoring: Tracks course engagement — enrolment, progress, completion rates, assessment scores, and feedback ratings. Flags employees with low engagement for manager awareness.

  Layer 5 — Effectiveness Measurement: At 30 and 90 days post-training, sends follow-up survey — behavioural change observed, skill application on the job. Links completion to performance improvement trend.

15
Succession Planning & Critical Role Coverage
PERFORMANCE & LEARNING


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Identifies critical roles, maps succession pipelines, assesses readiness of potential successors, and tracks development actions to close readiness gaps — ensuring business continuity for key positions.
  Prevents the company from being caught without leadership bench strength at critical moments.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Critical Role Identification: Analyses all roles and flags those that are critical — based on strategic importance, high difficulty to replace externally (time + cost), single-point-of-failure risk, and succession vacancy.

  Layer 2 — Successor Mapping: For each critical role, identifies 1-3 potential successors from the internal talent pool — using performance ratings, potential assessments, and competency match scores.

  Layer 3 — Readiness Assessment: Evaluates each potential successor's readiness — Now (can step in within 3 months), 1-2 years (with development), 3-5 years (early pipeline). Identifies readiness gaps.

  Layer 4 — Development Action Tracking: Defines specific development actions for each successor — stretch assignments, mentoring, external programmes. Tracks completion and progress against readiness milestones.

  Layer 5 — Retention Risk Overlay: Overlays flight risk data on the succession map. Flags critical roles where the current incumbent or the top successor is a retention risk — triggers emergency retention action.

Probation & Confirmation Management
PERFORMANCE & LEARNING


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Manages the probation period assessment process — triggering assessments at the right time, collecting structured manager and peer feedback, and recommending confirmation, extension, or separation.
  Ensures probation decisions are data-driven, fair, and legally documented.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Probation Calendar: Tracks all employees in probation (typically 3-6 months). Sends assessment alerts to the manager at Day 60 (early warning) and Day 90 (formal assessment).

  Layer 2 — Probation Assessment Form: Sends a structured probation assessment form to the line manager — performance against role KPIs, culture fit, attendance, and learning agility. Includes supporting evidence from onboarding check-ins.

  Layer 3 — Peer Input: Collects brief input from the buddy and key cross-functional partners on collaboration and integration. Aggregates with manager assessment.

  Layer 4 — HRBP Review: Routes the completed assessment to HRBP for review. HRBP checks for consistency with onboarding feedback and flags any assessments that seem inconsistent with earlier positive check-ins.

  Layer 5 — Decision & Communication: Based on the assessment, routes to one of three outcomes — Confirm (auto-confirm letter generated), Extend (30-day extension with specific targets), or Separate (HR manages the exit with legal review).

CB   Compensation & Benefits
Use Cases 17-21
Payroll, Annual Increments, Benefits, Variable Pay & Statutory Compliance


17
Payroll Processing & Reconciliation
COMPENSATION & BENEFITS


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Processes the full monthly payroll — computing gross-to-net for every employee including all earnings, deductions, statutory contributions, TDS, and off-cycle changes — and reconciles against the prior month and HRMS.
  Ensures 100% accurate, on-time payroll with complete statutory compliance and zero surprises.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Input Collection: Aggregates all payroll inputs — HRMS active employee list, approved compensation changes, leave and attendance data (LOP), expense reimbursements, variable pay, and statutory contribution rates.

  Layer 2 — Gross-to-Net Computation: Computes gross pay (basic + HRA + allowances + variable). Applies deductions — PF (employee 12% + employer 12%), ESI, professional tax, income tax (Section 192 TDS), and loan/advance recoveries.

  Layer 3 — Statutory Contribution Computation: Computes employer statutory contributions — PF (12% of basic), ESI (employer 3.25%), gratuity provision (4.81% of basic), and LWF. Generates ECR (Electronic Challan cum Return) for PF and ESI.

  Layer 4 — Variance Analysis: Compares payroll output against previous month. Flags variances above threshold — new joiners, exits, increments, LOP changes. Requires explanation for unexpected variances before processing.

  Layer 5 — Bank File & Reconciliation: Generates bank transfer file (NEFT/RTGS format) per employee. Reconciles payroll liability with Finance journal entry. Checks that net pay in bank file equals payroll register.

AGENT FLOW — STEP BY STEP

01
Input Agent
All payroll inputs collected
HRMS, attendance, reimbursements, variables compiled
02
Computation Agent

18
Annual Increment & Compensation Review
COMPENSATION & BENEFITS


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Manages the annual merit increase cycle — computing increment budgets, generating individual recommendations within approved bands, managing approvals, and implementing changes in payroll and HRMS.
  Ensures increment decisions are fair, defensible, differentiated by performance, and within budget.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Budget Computation: Reads the board-approved overall increment budget (% of total payroll). Distributes the budget by department and performance rating band — higher budgets for Exceeds Expectations, minimal for Below Expectations.

  Layer 2 — Individual Recommendation: Generates a recommended increment for each employee — combining performance rating weight, time since last increment, compa-ratio (current CTC vs. midpoint of grade band), and market benchmarks.

  Layer 3 — Manager Adjustment Window: Provides managers a window to adjust recommendations within their department budget — can increase one employee's increment but must reduce another's to stay within the approved envelope.

  Layer 4 — Budget Reconciliation: Continuously checks that manager adjustments stay within the approved department budget. Flags and blocks adjustments that would breach budget — manager must rebalance.

  Layer 5 — Implementation & Communication: After CHRO approval, updates all compensation records in HRMS and payroll (effective date). Generates increment letters per employee. Notifies managers to have increment conversations.

19
Benefits Administration
COMPENSATION & BENEFITS


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Manages the full employee benefits lifecycle — enrolment, claims, renewals, and vendor management for medical insurance, group life, accident cover, meal cards, and wellness benefits.
  Ensures employees maximise their benefit entitlements while the company minimises cost leakage and compliance gaps.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Enrolment Management: Triggers benefit enrolment at joining and during the annual open enrolment window. Communicates each benefit, its value, and enrolment deadline. Tracks enrolment completion rates.

  Layer 2 — Life Event Updates: Processes mid-year benefit changes triggered by life events — marriage (spouse added to Mediclaim), newborn (child added), divorce (removal), and employee death (nominee claim initiated).

  Layer 3 — Claims Assistance: Assists employees in filing insurance claims — collects documents, validates claim completeness, submits to insurer, and tracks claim status. Escalates delayed or rejected claims.

  Layer 4 — Utilisation Analytics: Tracks benefit utilisation rates per employee cohort — which benefits are used, which are not. Identifies benefits with < 20% utilisation for cost optimisation at renewal.

  Layer 5 — Renewal & Vendor Management: At policy renewal (typically annual), analyses claims data, market benchmarking, and utilisation rates. Prepares renewal brief for broker / HR — whether to renew, renegotiate, or switch.

20
Variable Pay & Incentive Management
COMPENSATION & BENEFITS


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Computes, approves, and processes all variable pay — annual bonuses, quarterly incentives, sales commissions, and retention bonuses — linked to company performance, business unit results, and individual ratings.
  Ensures pay-for-performance discipline with transparent, auditable, and on-time variable pay disbursement.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Plan Reading & Entitlement: Reads each employee's variable pay plan — target % of CTC, payout schedule (annual/quarterly), performance metrics, and minimum performance threshold for any payout.

  Layer 2 — Performance Multiplier: Applies company performance multiplier (e.g., 0.8x if company achieves 80% of target), business unit multiplier, and individual performance rating multiplier. Computes final payout formula.

  Layer 3 — Pro-Ration for Joiners/Exiters: Pro-rates variable pay for employees who joined or resigned mid-year. Applies policy rules — minimum months for eligibility, exit clause (resigned employees typically not eligible).

  Layer 4 — Approval Hierarchy: Routes variable pay computation to Finance for budget validation, then to CHRO for final approval. Flags cases where payout deviates significantly from target.

  Layer 5 — Disbursement & TDS: After approval, processes variable pay through payroll. Computes TDS on the additional income for the month. Generates payslip showing variable pay break-up and TDS impact.

21
Statutory Compliance — PF, ESI, Gratuity, LWF
COMPENSATION & BENEFITS


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Ensures 100% compliance with all statutory employment obligations — Provident Fund, ESI, Gratuity, Labour Welfare Fund, PT, Maternity Benefit, and Shops & Establishments — across all states of operation.
  Eliminates the significant penalty and prosecution risk from statutory non-compliance.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Applicability Matrix: Maps each establishment to all applicable labour laws — PF (all employees), ESI (employees earning ≤ ₹21,000/month), Gratuity (5+ years employees), and state-specific laws (PT, LWF rates vary by state).

  Layer 2 — Monthly ECR Filing (PF): Computes ECR — employer 12% + employee 12% of basic wage per member. Generates the ECR file. Monitors EPFO portal for challan generation and payment confirmation.

  Layer 3 — ESI Filing: Computes ESI contributions — employer 3.25% + employee 0.75% of gross wages for eligible employees. Generates half-yearly returns. Monitors ESIC portal.

  Layer 4 — Gratuity Provision & Payment: Computes gratuity entitlement for all eligible employees (15/26 × last drawn monthly salary × years of service). Maintains provision. Processes payment upon eligibility trigger (resignation, retirement, death).

  Layer 5 — Compliance Calendar: Maintains a statutory compliance calendar with all due dates. Sends 15-day advance reminders. Generates compliance score dashboard for CHRO and board.

POSH Compliance & Investigation Management
EMPLOYEE RELATIONS


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Manages the full POSH (Prevention of Sexual Harassment) compliance framework — ICC constitution, annual awareness training, complaint intake, investigation coordination, and report filing.
  Protects the company from legal liability while creating a safe, fair, and compliant workplace.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — ICC Compliance: Monitors ICC constitution — whether the Internal Committee is constituted as required by law (50% women, external member, 3-year terms). Triggers renewal alerts 60 days before term expiry.

  Layer 2 — Mandatory Training Tracking: Tracks POSH awareness training completion for all employees annually. Sends escalating reminders. Reports non-compliance to CHRO and flags for board reporting.

  Layer 3 — Complaint Intake: Provides a safe, confidential complaint intake channel — email, portal, or HR hotline. Acknowledges receipt within 24 hours. Assigns a complaint number and activates the ICC process.

  Layer 4 — Investigation Support: Creates an investigation file — complaint, respondent details, witness list, and hearing schedule. Tracks all investigation milestones against the 90-day statutory timeline.

  Layer 5 — Annual Report: Compiles the mandatory annual report to the District Officer — number of complaints received, disposed, pending, and details of each case. Generates the report in statutory format.

HR Policy Management & Compliance
EMPLOYEE RELATIONS


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Maintains the company's HR policy framework — ensuring policies are current, communicated, acknowledged, and consistently applied — and identifies compliance gaps across the workforce.
  Prevents HR-related legal exposure from outdated, unapplied, or unacknowledged policies.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Policy Currency Monitoring: Monitors all HR policies for relevance against current labour laws. Flags policies that need review due to legislative changes, court judgements, or business changes.

  Layer 2 — Review & Approval Workflow: For flagged policies, initiates the review — legal review, CHRO approval, and where required, works council / union consultation. Tracks the review to completion.

  Layer 3 — Policy Communication: Publishes updated policies on the HR portal. Sends mandatory read-and-acknowledge emails to all employees. Tracks acknowledgement rates. Sends reminders for non-acknowledgement.

  Layer 4 — Query Resolution: Handles employee queries on policy via an AI-powered HR chatbot. For complex or sensitive queries, routes to HRBP. Tracks query patterns to identify areas of policy confusion.

  Layer 5 — Compliance Audit: Periodically checks that policies are being consistently applied — reviewing leave approvals, disciplinary actions, and accommodation requests for policy adherence. Reports anomalies.

Grievance Management
EMPLOYEE RELATIONS


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Manages the formal employee grievance process — intake, classification, investigation, resolution, and escalation — ensuring every grievance is addressed fairly, confidentially, and within the prescribed timeline.
  Builds employee trust and prevents grievances from escalating to labour disputes or legal action.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Grievance Intake: Provides a confidential grievance submission channel. Receives grievance, acknowledges within 24 hours, assigns a tracking number, and classifies by type — compensation, promotion, harassment, workplace, manager behaviour.

  Layer 2 — Severity Classification: Assesses severity — Minor (policy query, process concern), Moderate (discrimination claim, manager bias), Major (harassment, wage theft, retaliation). Routes to appropriate level for resolution.

  Layer 3 — Investigation Coordination: For Moderate and Major grievances, assigns an HRBP investigator. Tracks investigation milestones, document collection, and both-party hearings against the 30-day resolution SLA.

  Layer 4 — Resolution & Communication: Documents the resolution decision with rationale. Communicates to the grievance-raiser with full explanation. Tracks whether the employee is satisfied with resolution or wishes to escalate.

  Layer 5 — Pattern Analytics: Analyses grievance patterns — which managers receive repeated grievances, which departments have high grievance rates, which issues are systemic. Reports to CHRO for proactive intervention.

25
Disciplinary Action Management
EMPLOYEE RELATIONS


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Manages disciplinary proceedings — from misconduct reporting through show cause, inquiry, and outcome — ensuring natural justice principles, policy consistency, and complete documentation.
  Protects the company from wrongful termination claims and ensures disciplinary actions are legally watertight.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Misconduct Report Intake: Reads a disciplinary report from a manager, HR, or compliance team. Classifies misconduct severity — Minor (absenteeism, late submission), Major (data theft, policy violation), Gross (fraud, harassment, violence).

  Layer 2 — Process Determination: Applies the appropriate process per severity — Minor (verbal warning → written warning), Major (show cause → inquiry committee), Gross (suspension → domestic inquiry → termination).

  Layer 3 — Show Cause Notice: Drafts and issues the Show Cause Notice (SCN) — specific allegations, evidence summary, and opportunity to respond within 7 working days. Tracks response receipt.

  Layer 4 — Inquiry Coordination: For Major/Gross misconduct, constitutes an inquiry committee. Manages the inquiry process — evidence submission, cross-examination, and final report. Tracks against statutory timelines.

  Layer 5 — Outcome & Documentation: Based on inquiry findings, supports the disciplinary authority in determining the outcome. Generates the outcome letter. Archives the complete file for statutory compliance and legal defence.

Labour Law & Regulatory Compliance
EMPLOYEE RELATIONS


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Monitors all applicable labour laws — the four Labour Codes (Wages, Industrial Relations, Social Security, Occupational Safety), state amendments, and registration requirements — across all establishments.
  Prevents prosecution, penalties, and reputational damage from labour law violations.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Establishment Mapping: Maps all company establishments (factories, offices, warehouses) to applicable labour laws — based on number of employees, nature of work, and state location.

  Layer 2 — Registration & Licence Monitoring: Tracks all labour law registrations and licences — Shops & Establishments, Factory Licence, Contract Labour Registration, Principal Employer registration. Monitors expiry and renewal.

  Layer 3 — Compliance Calendar: Maintains a comprehensive compliance calendar — all periodic returns (annual, half-yearly, quarterly, monthly) under applicable acts — by establishment and due date.

  Layer 4 — Return Preparation: Prepares periodic returns — Form A/B under Shops & Establishments, Annual Return under Factories Act, EPF/ESI returns. Validates data completeness before filing.

  Layer 5 — Labour Code Transition Monitoring: Tracks implementation notifications for the four Labour Codes. As codes come into force state by state, assesses the operational and policy changes required.

HR Audit & Compliance Reporting
EMPLOYEE RELATIONS


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Conducts periodic HR compliance audits — statutory registers, employment contracts, policy implementation, and process adherence — and prepares the HR section of the board compliance report.
  Provides the CHRO and board with confidence that HR operations are legally compliant across all dimensions.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Statutory Register Maintenance: Checks that all mandatory statutory registers are maintained correctly — Register of Employees, Register of Wages, Muster Roll, Register of Leaves, Accidents Register, and Register of Contractors.

  Layer 2 — Employment Contract Audit: Audits a sample of employment contracts — correct designation and CTC, appropriate notice period, IP assignment clauses, and GDPR / DPDP data processing consent clauses.

  Layer 3 — Process Adherence Audit: Reviews a sample of HR transactions — hiring approvals followed the correct process, increments within approved bands, disciplinary actions properly documented.

  Layer 4 — Policy Implementation Audit: Checks whether policies are being applied consistently — leave approvals within policy, accommodation requests treated equally, no systematic policy bypass by specific managers.

  Layer 5 — Board Compliance Report: Compiles the HR compliance section of the quarterly board report — statutory compliance status, open grievances, POSH matters, pending disciplinary cases, and headcount against plan.

HR Analytics & Workforce Planning
WORKFORCE ANALYTICS


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Provides the CHRO and leadership with data-driven workforce insights — headcount, attrition, diversity, cost, and productivity analytics — and models future workforce needs against strategic business plans.
  Transforms HR from reactive to predictive — enabling talent decisions based on evidence rather than intuition.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Headcount Analytics: Tracks live headcount against plan — total employees, by department, by grade, by location, and by employment type (permanent, contract, intern). Flags variance against approved plan.

  Layer 2 — Attrition Analytics: Computes attrition rate (voluntary, involuntary, total) by department, grade, tenure band, and manager. Identifies attrition hotspots and trends (improving vs. deteriorating).

  Layer 3 — Predictive Attrition Modelling: Applies machine learning to predict employees with high flight risk in the next 90 days — using features: tenure, last salary increase, recent feedback scores, manager change, and peer departures.

  Layer 4 — Diversity Analytics: Tracks workforce diversity — gender ratio by level, gender pay gap by grade, age diversity, and PwD representation. Compares against company targets and industry benchmarks.

  Layer 5 — Strategic Workforce Planning: Models future headcount needs — based on business growth plans, productivity benchmarks, and automation impact. Identifies future skill gaps and recommends build/buy/borrow strategy.

HR Technology & HRMS Administration
WORKFORCE ANALYTICS


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Manages the HR technology stack — HRMS configuration, integrations, data governance, user access, and system health — ensuring HR technology supports all HR processes without downtime or data quality issues.
  Prevents the costly operational failures and compliance risks that result from poorly maintained HR systems.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — HRMS Configuration Management: Monitors HRMS configuration for completeness — workflows, grade structures, compensation bands, leave policies, and organisation hierarchies. Flags configuration drift as business changes.

  Layer 2 — Integration Monitoring: Monitors all HRMS integrations — payroll, IT (Active Directory), Finance (cost centre), and attendance systems. Detects data sync failures and reconciliation breaks.

  Layer 3 — User Access Management: Manages HRMS user roles and access rights. Reviews access quarterly — removes access for separated employees, adjusts rights for transfers, and ensures no excessive access accumulation.

  Layer 4 — Data Quality Monitoring: Daily data quality checks — employee records with missing mandatory fields, organisation hierarchy breaks, duplicate employee codes, and cost centre mismatches.

  Layer 5 — Release & Change Management: Manages HRMS vendor updates and custom configurations — testing new releases in UAT, impact assessment, rollout coordination, and employee communication for interface changes.

HR Shared Services & Employee Self-Service
WORKFORCE ANALYTICS


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Operates HR Shared Services through an AI-powered service desk — handling employee queries, document requests, process guidance, and complaint routing — reducing HR team workload by 70%.
  Delivers consumer-grade HR service experience to all employees regardless of location or time zone.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Query Classification: Reads employee queries from chatbot, email, and HR portal. Classifies by topic — payroll (leave balance, payslip, TDS), benefits (Mediclaim, expense), processes (onboarding, transfer, increment), and policy.

  Layer 2 — FAQ Resolution: Resolves common, well-defined queries from the HR knowledge base — payslip download guidance, leave balance query, PF statement access, Form 16 availability, and policy interpretation.

  Layer 3 — Document Generation: Processes self-service requests — experience letter, salary certificate, employment certificate, relieving letter, Form 16, and No Objection Certificate — generating the document and delivering within 24 hours.

  Layer 4 — Complex Query Routing: For queries that require HR judgment — compensation concerns, manager complaints, policy exceptions — routes to the appropriate HRBP with full context. Tracks resolution and SLA.

  Layer 5 — Service Quality Monitoring: Tracks HR service desk metrics — query volume by category, first-contact resolution rate, average resolution time, and employee satisfaction. Identifies knowledge base gaps.

31
HR Budget Planning & Cost Management
WORKFORCE ANALYTICS


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Plans, tracks, and controls the HR budget — manpower costs (salaries, benefits, increments, variable pay), HR operational expenses, and talent acquisition costs — providing real-time HR cost intelligence to the CFO.
  Ensures HR costs are within plan and gives the CFO advance warning of budget risks.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Manpower Cost Projection: Projects total manpower cost — current payroll run rate plus approved increments, variable pay provisions, new hires in pipeline, and backfills in progress. Compares against annual budget.

  Layer 2 — Recruitment Cost Tracking: Tracks all talent acquisition costs — agency fees, job board subscriptions, background verification, relocation costs, and referral bonuses. Computes cost-per-hire by channel and level.

  Layer 3 — L&D Budget Tracking: Monitors learning and development spend against budget — external training programmes, e-learning licences, and conference attendance. Tracks cost-per-learning-hour.

  Layer 4 — Variance Analysis: Compares HR actual spend against plan monthly. Identifies overspends — reasons could be unplanned hiring, above-band increments, or higher-than-expected attrition driving higher recruitment costs.

  Layer 5 — CFO Reporting: Prepares the monthly HR cost report for the CFO — total manpower cost, headcount reconciliation, cost-per-employee trend, and projected full-year HR cost vs. budget.

Diversity, Equity & Inclusion (DEI) Monitoring
WORKFORCE ANALYTICS


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Tracks and reports on the company's DEI commitments — gender ratio, pay equity, inclusive hiring metrics, and representation at senior levels — and identifies actions to close equity gaps.
  Ensures DEI is measured, reported, and acted upon — not just aspirational.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — DEI Metrics Computation: Computes DEI metrics across the employee lifecycle — hiring (gender and diversity of shortlists and offers), promotions (women promoted at same rate as men), attrition (is there disparate attrition among minorities), pay equity.

  Layer 2 — Gender Pay Gap Analysis: Computes like-for-like gender pay gap — controlling for grade, function, and location. Identifies roles or departments with statistically significant pay differentials not explained by these factors.

  Layer 3 — Representation Tracking: Tracks representation vs. targets — gender in senior leadership (VP and above), PwD as % of workforce, and regional diversity. Compares against BRSR and ESG commitments.

  Layer 4 — Hiring Pipeline Diversity: Monitors diversity at each stage of the hiring funnel — sourcing (% diverse), shortlist (% diverse), interview (% diverse), offer (% diverse). Identifies where drop-off occurs.

  Layer 5 — Action Recommendation: Identifies specific, data-backed actions to close DEI gaps — diverse sourcing partnerships, blind resume screening, structured interview training, sponsorship programmes.

Employee Engagement Surveys & Action Planning
CULTURE & ENGAGEMENT


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Designs, deploys, and analyses employee engagement surveys — computing engagement scores, identifying drivers, benchmarking against industry, and generating manager-level action plans from the results.
  Turns survey data into specific, manager-driven actions that actually improve engagement.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Survey Design: Designs the engagement survey — validated questions across drivers (leadership, growth, recognition, work environment, belonging). Customises for the company context while maintaining benchmarkability.

  Layer 2 — Deployment & Participation: Deploys survey via email and mobile. Tracks participation rates by department and sends personalised reminders to non-respondents. Targets 80%+ participation for statistical validity.

  Layer 3 — Engagement Score Computation: Computes engagement scores — overall Engagement Index (eNPS), driver scores, and department / level / tenure cohort breakdowns. Applies statistical significance tests.

  Layer 4 — Insight Generation: Analyses verbatim comments using NLP — identifies top positive themes and top areas of concern. Correlates driver scores with attrition data to identify the engagement drivers that most predict retention.

  Layer 5 — Manager Action Planning: Generates a personalised action report for each manager — team engagement score, team's top 3 strengths and 3 improvement areas, and 3 specific recommended actions.

Recognition & Rewards Programme Management
CULTURE & ENGAGEMENT


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Manages the employee recognition programme — peer recognition, manager recognition, long service awards, and spot bonuses — ensuring recognition is frequent, genuine, timely, and financially controlled.
  Creates a culture of appreciation that improves engagement and retention without budget overruns.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Recognition Programme Design: Configures the recognition programme parameters — peer nomination rules, manager discretionary budget, recognition categories (innovation, collaboration, customer focus), and point/reward values.

  Layer 2 — Nomination Processing: Receives recognition nominations from peers and managers. Validates that nominations are specific, evidence-based, and linked to company values. Auto-approves peer recognitions within point budget.

  Layer 3 — Fairness Monitoring: Monitors recognition distribution for bias — are certain departments over- or under-nominated? Is recognition gender-balanced? Flags statistical anomalies to HRBP.

  Layer 4 — Awards & Communication: Processes approved recognitions — points awarded, certificate generated, shared on internal social wall (with employee consent), and integrated with payroll for taxable awards above threshold.

  Layer 5 — Budget Control: Tracks recognition spend against the quarterly budget per department. Alerts managers approaching budget limit. Reconciles recognition points redeemed vs. issued.

Wellness & Employee Assistance Programme
CULTURE & ENGAGEMENT


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Manages employee wellness initiatives — mental health resources, EAP (Employee Assistance Programme), physical wellness benefits, and work-life balance monitoring — identifying at-risk employees and facilitating support.
  Builds a psychologically safe, healthy workplace that reduces absenteeism and improves productivity.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Wellness Programme Management: Coordinates wellness calendar — physical health camps, mental health webinars, nutrition workshops, and step challenges. Tracks participation and engagement.

  Layer 2 — EAP Case Management: Manages employee referrals to the EAP provider — work stress, relationship issues, financial counselling, and mental health support. Maintains complete confidentiality; only aggregate data visible to HR.

  Layer 3 — Absence & Burnout Detection: Monitors absence patterns, overtime hours, and leave utilisation. Identifies employees showing burnout indicators — high overtime, declining leave utilisation, or frequent short sick leave.

  Layer 4 — Manager Wellness Enablement: Sends managers personalised dashboards showing their team's aggregate wellness indicators (not individual data). Provides managers with scripts for wellness conversations.

  Layer 5 — Wellness Analytics: Measures wellness programme ROI — participation rates, sick leave trends, EAP utilisation, and absenteeism cost reduction. Reports to CHRO for programme investment decisions.

HR Knowledge Management & Chatbot
CULTURE & ENGAGEMENT


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Maintains and continuously improves the HR knowledge base — policies, FAQs, process guides, and forms — powering an AI chatbot that handles 70% of employee HR queries without human intervention.
  Democratises HR knowledge access across all employees at any time, reducing HR team load and improving employee experience.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Knowledge Base Curation: Maintains the HR knowledge base — policy documents, process guides, FAQs, forms, and how-to videos. Auto-detects when policies are updated and refreshes corresponding KB articles.

  Layer 2 — Query Understanding: Uses NLP to understand the intent behind employee questions — even when poorly phrased or multi-part. Maps intent to the most relevant knowledge base article or process.

  Layer 3 — Personalised Response: Tailors responses to the employee's context — their leave balance, benefits plan, grade level, and location. A query about 'my maternity leave entitlement' returns the employee's specific entitlement, not a generic policy.

  Layer 4 — Escalation Intelligence: Identifies when an employee needs human support — emotional distress signals, sensitive HR matters, or queries outside the knowledge base. Escalates to HRBP with full conversation context.

  Layer 5 — Continuous Improvement: Analyses queries that were escalated, poorly answered, or highly rated — to improve KB articles and chatbot responses. Identifies emerging query trends signalling policy confusion or employee concerns.

CE Control Environment & Governance
Use Cases 01-05
Control Design & Effectiveness, Authority Matrix Validation, Delegation of Authority, Policy Enforcement & Organizational Structure
01
Control Design & Effectiveness Assessment
CONTROL ENVIRONMENT & GOVERNANCE
WHAT THE AGENT DOES
Continuously evaluates the design and operating effectiveness of internal controls across the organization, ensuring controls are properly designed to mitigate identified risks and are functioning as intended in day-to-day operations.
REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS
Layer 1 — Control Inventory & Mapping: Maintains a complete inventory of all financial controls across processes (revenue, procurement, inventory, payroll, treasury, financial reporting). Maps each control to specific risks, process steps, and responsible owners.
Layer 2 — Design Effectiveness Analysis: Evaluates whether controls are properly designed to address identified risks. Assesses control frequency, nature (preventive vs. detective), automation level, and coverage gaps.
Layer 3 — Operating Effectiveness Monitoring: Tracks control execution in real-time through system logs, approvals, reconciliations, and exception reports. Identifies missed controls, delayed executions, or pattern deviations.
Layer 4 — Control Deficiency Detection: Flags design deficiencies (controls that cannot effectively mitigate the risk) and operating deficiencies (controls not executed as designed). Categorizes severity as significant, material, or minor.
Layer 5 — Remediation Tracking: Monitors management action plans for identified deficiencies, tracks implementation status, validates corrective actions, and updates control documentation.
AGENT FLOW — STEP BY STEP
01
Control Inventory Agent
Complete control catalog maintained
All financial controls mapped to risks and owners
02
Design Review Agent
Control design evaluated against risk
Design gaps and redundancies identified
03
Execution Monitor Agent
Real-time control execution tracked
Operating effectiveness continuously monitored
04
Deficiency Detection Agent
Control failures and gaps flagged
Severity categorized; owners notified
05
Remediation Agent
Management action plans tracked
Remediation status validated and documented
06
Documentation Agent
Control testing evidence compiled
Audit-ready documentation maintained
07
Reporting Agent
Control effectiveness dashboard updated
Real-time visibility to management and auditors

02
Authority Matrix & Approval Rights Validation
CONTROL ENVIRONMENT & GOVERNANCE
WHAT THE AGENT DOES
Validates that all financial transactions and decisions are approved by individuals with appropriate authority levels as defined in the delegation of authority matrix, preventing unauthorized transactions and ensuring proper segregation of duties.
REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS
Layer 1 — Authority Matrix Maintenance: Maintains the current state of approval authorities across all financial processes including purchase requisitions, purchase orders, vendor payments, journal entries, payroll changes, and bank transactions.
Layer 2 — Transaction Authorization Validation: Checks every financial transaction against the authority matrix before processing. Validates approver's designation, approval amount limits, and transaction type permissions.
Layer 3 — Segregation of Duties Enforcement: Ensures that individuals initiating transactions cannot approve them, those approving cannot record, and those with custody cannot reconcile. Flags violations in real-time.
Layer 4 — Exception Handling & Override Detection: Identifies transactions processed without proper approval, emergency overrides, or circumvention of approval workflows. Flags for management review.
Layer 5 — Authority Change Management: Tracks changes to the authority matrix (new joiners, role changes, resignations, temporary delegations) and ensures system access rights are updated immediately.
AGENT FLOW — STEP BY STEP
01
Matrix Sync Agent
Authority matrix updated continuously
Real-time sync with HR and role changes
02
Transaction Validator Agent
Every transaction checked against authority
Approval rights validated before processing
03
SOD Monitor Agent
Segregation of duties violations detected
Conflicting roles and dual authorities flagged
04
Exception Agent
Unauthorized transactions identified
Override attempts and bypasses flagged
05
Access Control Agent
System access aligned with authority matrix
Orphaned access rights removed
06
Audit Trail Agent
Complete approval trail maintained
Timestamped evidence of all approvals
07
Escalation Agent
Violations escalated to management
Authority breaches reviewed and resolved

03
Delegation of Authority Compliance Monitoring
CONTROL ENVIRONMENT & GOVERNANCE
WHAT THE AGENT DOES
Ensures that all delegated authorities are properly documented, time-bound, within approved limits, and revoked when no longer needed, maintaining a clear chain of accountability for all financial decisions.
REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS
Layer 1 — Delegation Registry: Maintains a complete record of all active delegations including temporary approvals during leave, project-specific authorities, and emergency delegation scenarios.
Layer 2 — Delegation Validity Check: Validates that delegations are properly approved by authorized individuals, documented with clear scope and limits, and time-bound with expiry dates.
Layer 3 — Auto-Revocation Management: Automatically revokes delegations when the specified period expires, when the delegating officer returns, or when the delegated individual changes roles.
Layer 4 — Delegation Limit Enforcement: Ensures delegated authorities do not exceed the delegating officer's own limits and validates that delegation is within permissible scope.
Layer 5 — Abuse & Pattern Detection: Identifies patterns of frequent delegation, delegations just before year-end, or delegation chains that circumvent proper oversight.
AGENT FLOW — STEP BY STEP
01
Delegation Registry Agent
All delegations centrally tracked
Complete audit trail of delegation history
02
Approval Validator Agent
Delegation requests validated before approval
Limits and scope checked against policy
03
Expiry Monitor Agent
Delegations auto-revoked on expiry
System access removed on scheduled date
04
Limit Enforcement Agent
Delegated amounts within approved limits
Excess delegation attempts blocked
05
Pattern Detection Agent
Abuse patterns identified
Suspicious delegation behavior flagged
06
Notification Agent
Expiry alerts sent before delegation ends
Renewal or handover process initiated
07
Compliance Agent
Delegation compliance dashboard updated
Real-time view of active delegations

04
Policy & Procedure Enforcement Automation
CONTROL ENVIRONMENT & GOVERNANCE
WHAT THE AGENT DOES
Automatically enforces financial policies and accounting procedures across all transactions and processes, ensuring consistent application of company standards and regulatory requirements without relying on manual oversight.
REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS
Layer 1 — Policy Rules Engine: Maintains all financial policies as executable rules including expense limits, vendor selection criteria, payment terms, credit approval criteria, capitalization thresholds, and revenue recognition rules.
Layer 2 — Real-Time Policy Validation: Validates every transaction against applicable policies before processing. Blocks transactions that violate mandatory policies and flags those requiring management exception.
Layer 3 — Exception Management Workflow: Routes policy violations to appropriate exception approvers, documents justification, tracks exception patterns, and maintains exception register for audit review.
Layer 4 — Policy Version Control: Maintains complete history of policy changes, tracks effective dates, manages transition periods, and ensures all transactions are validated against the correct policy version.
Layer 5 — Compliance Gap Analysis: Identifies processes, systems, or user groups with high policy violation rates, analyzes root causes, and recommends policy updates or training interventions.
AGENT FLOW — STEP BY STEP

Policy Engine Agent
All policies loaded as validation rules
Real-time policy library maintained

Violations blocked or flagged for exception

Version Control Agent
