Solution Document Generator Agent
PRESALES & SOLUTION ENGINEERING
WHAT THE AGENT DOES
Generates comprehensive solution documents including solution overview, architecture diagrams, technical specifications, implementation approach, and delivery methodology customized to customer requirements and industry best practices.
REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS
Layer 1 — Requirement Analysis & Solution Design: Analyzes customer requirements, technical constraints, integration needs, and success criteria. Designs solution architecture addressing functional and non-functional requirements. Selects appropriate components, technologies, and deployment models.
Layer 2 — Architecture Diagram Generation: Creates solution architecture diagrams including high-level architecture, component diagrams, data flow diagrams, and integration architecture. Uses standard notation (UML, C4 model) and generates diagrams programmatically.
Layer 3 — Technical Specification Authoring: Generates detailed technical specifications including system requirements, interface specifications, data models, security architecture, and performance characteristics. Ensures specifications align with architecture and requirements.
Layer 4 — Implementation Approach Definition: Defines implementation methodology, phasing strategy, migration approach, testing strategy, and deployment plan. Identifies risks, dependencies, and critical success factors. Creates implementation timeline and resource plan.
Layer 5 — Document Assembly & Formatting: Assembles solution document with professional formatting including executive summary, solution overview, architecture, specifications, implementation plan, and appendices. Ensures consistency, readability, and professional presentation.
AGENT FLOW — STEP BY STEP
01
Requirements Agent
Customer requirements analyzed
Functional and technical needs identified

WHAT THE AGENT DOES
Validates proposal completeness by checking all RFP requirements addressed, mandatory sections included, compliance criteria met, formatting correct, and supporting materials attached before submission.
REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS
Layer 1 — Requirement Coverage Analysis: Cross-references proposal content against RFP requirements ensuring every question answered, every requirement addressed, and all mandatory sections included. Identifies gaps and missing responses.
Layer 2 — Compliance Validation: Validates compliance with RFP submission requirements including page limits, font specifications, file formats, naming conventions, and submission portal requirements. Checks for disqualifying omissions or errors.
Layer 3 — Supporting Documentation Verification: Verifies all required supporting documents attached including financial statements, certifications, insurance certificates, references, case studies, and technical documentation. Ensures documents are current and complete.
Layer 4 — Formatting & Presentation Quality: Checks formatting consistency including fonts, headers, page numbers, table of contents, and professional presentation. Validates diagram quality, readability, and proper labeling.
Layer 5 — Pre-Submission Checklist: Generates submission checklist including all items requiring verification before submission. Tracks completion status and sends reminders for pending items. Provides final go/no-go recommendation.

GSTR-1, GSTR-3B, GSTR-2B Reconciliation, Annual Return, Refunds, E-Invoice & Notice Management


01
GSTR-1 Outward Supply Return Preparation
GOODS & SERVICES TAX


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Aggregates all outward supply data for the month — B2B invoices, B2C transactions, exports, credit notes, and debit notes — and auto-prepares the GSTR-1 return with accurate HSN summary, document totals, and amendment schedules.
  Eliminates the manual compilation effort that typically consumes 3-5 days of the tax team every month.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Sales Register Extraction: Reads all outward supply transactions from ERP — tax invoices, credit notes, debit notes, export invoices, bill of supply, and receipt vouchers — for the return period.

  Layer 2 — Supply Classification: Classifies each transaction by supply type — B2B (with GSTIN), B2C large (> ₹2.5 lakh), B2C small, exports (with/without payment of tax), exempt/nil-rated, and non-GST supplies.

  Layer 3 — GSTIN Validation: Validates counterparty GSTINs against the GSTN database in real time. Flags invalid, cancelled, or mismatched GSTINs before return filing to prevent ITC mismatch for counterparties.

  Layer 4 — HSN Summary Computation: Aggregates turnover and tax by HSN/SAC code. Validates that HSN detail is complete for all applicable transactions (mandatory for annual turnover > ₹5 Cr).

  Layer 5 — Amendment Detection: Identifies transactions from prior periods that require amendment in the current GSTR-1 (Tables 9A, 9B, 9C). Flags the delta and generates the amendment schedule.

AGENT FLOW — STEP BY STEP

Sales Register Agent
ERP outward supply data extracted
All supply transactions for the period compiled
02
Classification Agent
Supply type determined per transaction
B2B/B2C/export/exempt classification applied
03
GSTIN Validation Agent
All counterparty GSTINs validated
Invalid GSTINs flagged; supplier notified for correction
04
HSN Agent

Turnover and tax by HSN/SAC code computed
05
Amendment Agent

06

Any variance flagged for review
07
Filing Agent

Return package ready for GSP upload and filing

CONFIDENCE-BASED ROUTING

Confidence
Action
Human Effort
> 95% data accuracy

IT + tax team action

Outcome:  GSTR-1 preparation from 3-5 days to 4 hours. GSTIN validation errors eliminated before filing. ITC mismatch notices to customers reduced 80%.


02
GSTR-3B Computation & Self-Assessment
GOODS & SERVICES TAX


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Computes the GSTR-3B monthly summary return — output tax liability, eligible ITC, ITC reversal, and net tax payable — with full reconciliation against GSTR-1 and GSTR-2B, identifying any discrepancies before filing.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Output Tax Liability: Aggregates GSTR-1 data to compute Table 3.1 output tax liability — taxable supplies (IGST/CGST/SGST/Cess), zero-rated, exempt, and non-GST supplies.

  Layer 2 — ITC Computation: Reads GSTR-2B auto-populated ITC. Applies eligibility filters — business use test, Section 17(5) blocked credit exclusion, RCM credits, and import IGST credits.

  Layer 3 — ITC Reversal Computation: Computes mandatory ITC reversals — Rule 42 (exempt/non-business use proportion), Rule 43 (capital goods for exempt use), and Rule 37 (non-payment to supplier within 180 days).

  Layer 4 — Net Tax Payable: Computes net GST payable — Output Tax minus Eligible ITC minus ITC reversals. Applies credit ledger utilisation in correct order (IGST first, then CGST/SGST).

  Layer 5 — GSTR-1 vs. GSTR-3B Reconciliation: Reconciles GSTR-3B liability against GSTR-1 declared supplies. Flags any variance — must be within CBIC tolerance limits to avoid SCN.

AGENT FLOW — STEP BY STEP

01
Liability Agent
GSTR-1 output tax aggregated
Table 3.1 liability by IGST/CGST/SGST computed
02
ITC Agent
GSTR-2B ITC extracted and filtered
Eligible ITC after blocked credit exclusion computed
03
ITC Reversal Agent
Mandatory reversals computed
Rule 42/43/37 reversals calculated
04
Netting Agent
Net tax payable computed
Credit ledger utilisation applied in correct sequence
05
Reconciliation Agent
GSTR-1 vs. GSTR-3B variance checked
Within-tolerance confirmation or variance flag raised
06
Interest Computation Agent
Late payment interest computed if applicable
Interest at 18% p.a. for any delayed periods computed
07
Filing Package Agent
GSTR-3B data compiled
JSON / portal entry ready for review and filing

CONFIDENCE-BASED ROUTING

Confidence
Action
Human Effort
> 99% match with GSTR-1

Net refund position

Refund process

Outcome:  GSTR-3B computation from 2 days to 3 hours. ITC reversal errors eliminated. GSTR-1 vs. GSTR-3B mismatch notices avoided.


03
GSTR-2B Reconciliation & ITC Optimisation
GOODS & SERVICES TAX


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Reconciles the auto-generated GSTR-2B (supplier-filed ITC) against the company's purchase register — identifying missing ITC, supplier non-filers, and ITC at risk — maximising eligible credit claims.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — GSTR-2B Data Download: Fetches the GSTR-2B statement from the GST portal for the period. Extracts all ITC available — B2B invoices, import IGST, RCM inward supplies, and ISD credits.

  Layer 2 — Purchase Register Matching: Matches GSTR-2B entries against the company's purchase register line by line — GSTIN + Invoice No + Date + Amount. Classifies each as: Matched, Invoice Date Mismatch, Amount Mismatch, or Book entry not in GSTR-2B.

  Layer 3 — Mismatch Classification: For each mismatch, determines the root cause — supplier has not yet filed GSTR-1, supplier filed with wrong invoice number, supplier filed with wrong amount, or genuine dispute.

  Layer 4 — Supplier Communication: For invoices in books but not in GSTR-2B, generates supplier communication — requesting the supplier to file GSTR-1 correctly or amend the filed return.

  Layer 5 — ITC Risk Quantification: Computes ITC at risk — amounts where supplier has not filed and ITC cannot be claimed beyond the cap (currently 105% of GSTR-2B ITC). Flags for CFO provisioning decision.

AGENT FLOW — STEP BY STEP

GSTR-2B Agent
Portal data downloaded
GSTR-2B entries for period extracted

Purchase Register Agent

Purchase register entries for period extracted

Matching Agent

Root Cause Agent

Supplier filing issue vs. book entry error identified

ITC not claimable due to supplier non-filing computed

ITC Claim Agent

04
GST Annual Return (GSTR-9) & Reconciliation Statement (GSTR-9C)
GOODS & SERVICES TAX


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Prepares the annual GST return GSTR-9 and, where applicable, the reconciliation statement GSTR-9C — reconciling the annual audited financials against GST returns filed during the year.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Annual Data Compilation: Aggregates all 12 months of GSTR-1 and GSTR-3B data. Builds the annual supply and ITC summary. Validates that monthly returns are consistent with each other.

  Layer 2 — Books vs. Returns Reconciliation: Reconciles annual turnover and tax in GST returns against audited financial statements. Identifies differences — timing differences (advance receipts), classification differences, and genuine omissions.

  Layer 3 — ITC Annual Reconciliation: Reconciles ITC claimed in GSTR-3B across the year against GSTR-2A/2B and books. Identifies excess ITC claimed (reversal needed) and unclaimed eligible ITC (claim opportunity).

  Layer 4 — HSN Turnover Summary: Aggregates annual turnover and tax by HSN code across all monthly returns. Validates against books and resolves classification inconsistencies.

  Layer 5 — Auditor Collaboration: For GSTR-9C, prepares the reconciliation statement with difference explanations, tax liability on unreported/under-reported supplies, and auditor sign-off package.

05
GST Refund Application Management
GOODS & SERVICES TAX


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Identifies and quantifies GST refund entitlements — export refunds, inverted duty structure refunds, and excess ITC refunds — and manages the application, tracking, and deficiency memo response.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Refund Entitlement Identification: Scans the tax position for refund scenarios — exports with accumulated ITC (LUT route), inverted duty structure where input tax rate exceeds output rate, and excess cash in electronic ledger.

  Layer 2 — Net ITC Computation: For inverted duty refunds, computes the eligible refund using the prescribed formula — (Turnover of inverted supplies / Total turnover) × Net ITC for the period.

  Layer 3 — Export Refund Validation: For export refunds, matches shipping bills against GSTR-1 export invoices. Validates FIRC/BRC receipt for export proceeds. Flags discrepancies.

  Layer 4 — Application Preparation: Prepares Form RFD-01 with all required annexures — statement of invoices, computation of refund, bank details, undertaking, and supporting documents.

  Layer 5 — Deficiency Memo Handling: When the GST officer issues a deficiency memo (Form RFD-03), reads the objections, prepares responses with additional documents, and refiles within the prescribed window.

AGENT FLOW — STEP BY STEP

Refund Identification Agent

Export / inverted duty / excess cash refund quantified

Conditions precedent verified (LUT valid, exports confirmed)

Application Agent

06
E-Invoice Compliance & IRN Management
GOODS & SERVICES TAX


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Automates the e-invoice generation, IRN issuance, QR code embedding, and real-time validation process — ensuring 100% compliance with the mandatory e-invoicing regime for applicable taxpayers.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Invoice Eligibility Check: Determines which invoices require e-invoicing — based on taxpayer turnover threshold, supply type (B2B/exports/reverse charge), and transaction nature. Excludes SEZ, banking, insurance, and other exempt categories.

  Layer 2 — Schema Validation: Validates invoice data against the IRP (Invoice Registration Portal) schema before transmission — mandatory fields, format compliance, GSTIN validity, and HSN code correctness.

  Layer 3 — IRN Generation: Transmits validated invoice data to the IRP via API. Receives and stores the IRN (Invoice Reference Number), signed e-invoice JSON, and QR code.

  Layer 4 — PDF Embedding & Distribution: Embeds the QR code and IRN on the PDF invoice. Routes the e-invoice to the customer via the correct channel — email, portal, EDI.

  Layer 5 — Cancellation & Amendment Management: For invoices that need to be cancelled (within 24-hour window), manages the IRP cancellation API call. For post-24-hour corrections, issues credit/debit notes with reference to original IRN.

AGENT FLOW — STEP BY STEP

Eligibility Agent

Applicable invoices flagged for IRP submission

Validation Agent

Field completeness and format compliance confirmed

Outcome:  100% e-invoice compliance. IRN generation in < 2 seconds per invoice. Zero penalty risk from e-invoice non-compliance. IRP failure handled gracefully.


07
GST Notice Management & Response
GOODS & SERVICES TAX


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Reads, classifies, and manages all GST notices — ASMT-10, DRC-01, SCN, audit notices — computing tax liabilities, preparing detailed responses with evidence, and tracking proceedings through closure.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Notice Classification: Reads the notice from the GST portal or email. Classifies by type — scrutiny (ASMT-10), DRC-01 (demand after audit/investigation), SCN (Show Cause Notice), and audit/inspection notices.

  Layer 2 — Issue Identification: Extracts the specific issues raised — ITC mismatch, turnover mismatch, classification dispute, rate dispute, reverse charge non-compliance, or place of supply error.

  Layer 3 — Evidence Compilation: Retrieves all relevant evidence from ERP and document management — invoices, GRNs, contracts, payment proofs, GSTR filings, and reconciliations — to support or challenge the demand.

  Layer 4 — Tax Liability Computation: Computes the correct tax liability per the company's legal position. Calculates tax, interest (Section 50), and penalty (Section 73/74) amounts if any portion of demand is conceded.

  Layer 5 — Response Drafting: Prepares a detailed reply — legal grounds, supporting evidence, reconciliation schedules, and computation of admitted vs. disputed liability. Routes for review and filing within the response window.

AGENT FLOW — STEP BY STEP

Notice Intake Agent

Computation Agent

Admitted and disputed amounts quantified

07
Filing Agent

Acknowledgement obtained; next deadline tracked

Confidence
Action
Human Effort

IT   Income Tax
Use Cases 08-13
Advance Tax, ITR-6, TDS Compliance, 26AS Reconciliation, Transfer Pricing & Litigation


08
Advance Tax Computation & Payment
INCOME TAX


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Computes quarterly advance tax liability based on year-to-date income, expenses, deductions, and tax credits — ensuring instalments are accurate and paid by due dates to avoid interest under Sections 234B and 234C.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Year-to-Date P&L Extraction: Reads the YTD profit & loss account from the accounting system. Extracts revenue, expenses, depreciation, and other income components relevant to taxable income computation.

  Layer 2 — Taxable Income Projection: Projects the full-year taxable income — adjusting YTD actuals for: remaining period projections, disallowable expenses (Section 40a, 40A), deductions (80IC, 80JJAA, 80M), and losses brought forward.

  Layer 3 — Tax Liability Computation: Applies the correct tax rate (base rate + surcharge + cess) to projected taxable income. Computes MAT liability (Section 115JB) and compares with normal tax — higher of the two applies.

  Layer 4 — Instalment Computation: Computes the advance tax instalment due — based on the percentage schedule (15%/45%/75%/100% by June/September/December/March) less TDS already deducted and previous instalments paid.

  Layer 5 — Payment Authorisation & Challan: Prepares Challan ITNS 280 with correct assessment year, tax type, and amount. Routes for CFO authorisation and initiates bank payment. Archives paid challan.

AGENT FLOW — STEP BY STEP

01
P&L Extraction Agent
YTD P&L data read from accounting system
Income and expense components extracted
02
Projection Agent
Full-year taxable income projected
Actuals + estimates for remaining period computed
03
Tax Rate Agent
Applicable tax rate and surcharge applied
Normal tax and MAT computed; higher liability selected
04
TDS Credit Agent
TDS deducted and deposited so far read
Available TDS credit against advance tax computed
05
Instalment Agent
Due instalment computed
Amount to pay this quarter determined
06
Challan Agent
Challan ITNS 280 prepared
Assessment year, major head, and amount populated
07
Payment Agent
Authorisation workflow triggered
Payment initiated; receipt archived; due dates updated

CONFIDENCE-BASED ROUTING

Confidence
Action
Human Effort
Clear computation, sufficient funds
Challan prepared; CFO approves; payment made
CFO approval < 10 min
Significant income spike vs. prior quarter
Revised projection presented; instalment adjustment
CFO + tax discussion
MAT higher than normal tax
MAT credit (Section 115JAA) computation prepared
Tax team review
Insufficient cash for payment
Treasury alert; alternative funding plan
CFO + treasury action

Outcome:  Advance tax always paid by due dates — zero Section 234B/234C interest. Quarterly computation from 2 days to 4 hours. CFO visibility of tax liability improved.


09
Income Tax Return (ITR) Preparation
INCOME TAX


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Prepares the corporate income tax return (ITR-6) — computing total income, tax liability, Schedule AL, Schedule BP, and all required disclosures — from the audited financial statements and supporting tax workings.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Financial Statements Reading: Reads audited P&L, balance sheet, and notes. Extracts all income and expenditure line items and maps them to the relevant ITR schedule heads.

  Layer 2 — Tax Computation: Applies income tax adjustments to book profit — adds back disallowances (Section 40, 40A, 43B), reduces exempt income (Section 10), applies deductions (Chapter VIA), and computes taxable income under each head.

  Layer 3 — Schedule BP (Business Profit): Completes Schedule BP — book profit as per P&L, adjustments for depreciation under Section 32, provisions disallowed under Section 43B, and 80-IC/J/JJA deductions.

  Layer 4 — MAT / AMT Computation: Computes book profit under Section 115JB, applies MAT rate, and claims MAT credit (Section 115JAA) from prior years. Computes AMT where applicable.

  Layer 5 — Tax Credit & Self-Assessment: Aggregates TDS credits (Form 26AS / AIS), advance tax paid, and MAT credit. Computes self-assessment tax payable / refund due. Prepares all schedules and XML/JSON for e-filing.

10
TDS Compliance — Deduction, Deposit & Returns
INCOME TAX


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Manages the complete TDS compliance cycle — determining the correct TDS section and rate for every payment, verifying PAN, computing threshold accumulation, deducting, depositing, and filing returns (26Q/27Q/27EQ).

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Payment Nature Classification: Reads each vendor payment from the accounts payable system. Classifies the nature of payment — professional fees (194J), contract (194C), rent (194I), interest (194A), commission (194H), salary (192), etc.

  Layer 2 — TDS Section & Rate Determination: Maps payment nature to the correct TDS section. Applies the applicable rate — standard rate, reduced rate (lower deduction certificate), or higher rate (Section 206AA for PAN-less payees).

  Layer 3 — Threshold Accumulation Tracking: Tracks cumulative payments to each vendor under each TDS section across the financial year. Triggers TDS deduction only when the aggregate crosses the statutory threshold.

  Layer 4 — PAN & TRACES Validation: Validates vendor PAN against the Income Tax PAN database. Checks TRACES for lower deduction certificates (Form 13). Applies 20% TDS for invalid/missing PAN (Section 206AA).

  Layer 5 — Challan, Return & Certificate: Generates Challan ITNS 281 for TDS deposit. Compiles data for quarterly return (26Q/27Q/27EQ) using RPU. Generates TDS certificates (Form 16A/16/27D) for deductees.

11
Form 26AS / AIS Reconciliation
INCOME TAX


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Reconciles the company's books of account against Form 26AS (TDS/TCS credit statement) and AIS (Annual Information Statement) — ensuring all tax credits are captured and no transactions are unreported.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — 26AS Data Download: Downloads the current Form 26AS and AIS from the TRACES / Compliance Portal. Extracts all TDS credits (by deductor), TCS collected, advance tax paid, and refunds received.

  Layer 2 — Books Matching — TDS Credits: Matches each TDS credit in 26AS against the corresponding income entry in the books. Identifies income that was received (and TDS deducted) but not reflected in books — risk of omission.

  Layer 3 — Books Matching — Payments: Matches advance tax and self-assessment tax challan entries in 26AS against the company's tax payment records. Ensures no challan is unmatched or misallocated.

  Layer 4 — AIS Verification: Reviews the AIS for transactions flagged by the Income Tax Department — high-value transactions, SFT (Statement of Financial Transaction) data from banks, MCA filings. Validates each against books.

  Layer 5 — Feedback Submission: For AIS entries that are incorrect (wrong amount, wrong entity, or already reported elsewhere), submits online feedback on the Compliance Portal to correct the AIS before it impacts assessment.

12
Transfer Pricing Documentation & Compliance
INCOME TAX


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Monitors all international and domestic transactions between related parties, computes arm's length pricing, prepares TP documentation (Form 3CEB), and manages TP audit responses.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Related Party Transaction Identification: Reads all intercompany transactions from the ERP — loans, service fees, royalties, goods sales, guarantees, and cost allocations — across all group entities and geographies.

  Layer 2 — Materiality & Reporting Threshold: Checks whether transactions cross the reporting thresholds — international transactions (any amount), specified domestic transactions (> ₹20 Cr). Determines which need TP study.

  Layer 3 — Method Selection & Benchmarking: Selects the most appropriate TP method — CUP, CPM, TNMM, PSM, or RPM — based on transaction type and comparability analysis. Runs benchmarking database search for comparable uncontrolled transactions.

  Layer 4 — Arm's Length Range Computation: Computes the arm's length price (ALP) and acceptable range. Validates whether the actual transaction price falls within the ALP range. Flags transactions outside the range.

  Layer 5 — Form 3CEB & TP Report: Prepares Form 3CEB (Accountant's Report) and the comprehensive TP documentation report — benchmarking analysis, functional analysis, economic analysis, and year-end adjustment workings.

Income Tax Assessment & Litigation Management
INCOME TAX


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Manages income tax assessment proceedings from the intimation/notice stage through scrutiny, CIT(A), ITAT, and higher court stages — tracking demands, deposits, and stay applications across all litigation matters.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Assessment Notice Management: Reads all assessment notices from the Compliance Portal — Section 143(1) intimations, Section 143(2) scrutiny notices, Section 148 reopening notices, and Section 263/264 revision notices.

  Layer 2 — Assessment Order Analysis: Reads assessment orders, computes additions/disallowances made by the assessing officer, and identifies each issue — facts, legal grounds, and quantum.

  Layer 3 — Demand Computation & Verification: Verifies the demand computation in the assessment order. Identifies computational errors. Computes the correct demand including interest under Section 234A/234B/234C.

  Layer 4 — Appeal Strategy: For each addition, evaluates the legal strength of the company's position — favourable judicial precedents, CBDT circulars, and case-specific facts. Recommends appeal or acceptance per issue.

  Layer 5 — Litigation Tracking: Maintains a complete litigation register — all open assessment years, issues in dispute, amounts, current stage (CIT(A)/ITAT/HC/SC), expected hearing dates, and provisions made in books.

14
TCS (Tax Collected at Source) Compliance
TDS / TCS COMPLIANCE


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Manages TCS compliance for all applicable transactions — sale of goods (Section 206C(1H)), foreign remittances, overseas tour packages, alcohol, timber, tendu leaves, and scrap — with auto-collection, deposit, and return filing.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — TCS Applicability Determination: Reads each sale/collection transaction. Determines TCS applicability — Section 206C(1H) for goods sales exceeding ₹50 lakh threshold per buyer, foreign remittances > ₹7 lakh (LRS), and specified goods.

  Layer 2 — Threshold Tracking: Tracks cumulative receipts from each buyer under Section 206C(1H). Triggers TCS collection only after the ₹50 lakh threshold is crossed for that buyer in the financial year.

  Layer 3 — TCS Rate Application: Applies the correct TCS rate — 0.1% for Section 206C(1H), higher rates for foreign remittances (5%/0.5%), specified goods (various). Applies 5% higher rate (206CC) if PAN not provided.

  Layer 4 — Receipt Monitoring: Monitors all receipts (not invoices) from buyer. TCS under Section 206C(1H) is triggered on receipt — so actual collection triggers the liability, not the invoice date.

  Layer 5 — Deposit & Return: Generates Challan ITNS 281 for TCS deposit. Compiles quarterly TCS return (27EQ) using RPU. Generates TCS certificate (Form 27D) for buyers.

Collection Agent

Salary TDS (Section 192) & Form 16 Compliance
TDS / TCS COMPLIANCE


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Computes monthly salary TDS for every employee — accounting for all income sources, deductions, exemptions, and regime choice — generating Form 16 Parts A and B at year end.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Salary Structure Reading: Reads employee salary structures from HRMS — basic, HRA, special allowance, LTA, reimbursements, bonuses, and ESOP perquisites. Applies tax exemptions (Section 10) — HRA, LTA, gratuity, leave encashment.

  Layer 2 — Regime Selection: Determines the employee's tax regime choice — old (with deductions) or new (without deductions, lower rates). Applies the chosen regime throughout the year and flags if it can be changed.

  Layer 3 — Deduction Computation (Old Regime): For employees on old regime, computes all eligible deductions — Section 80C (PF, ELSS, LIC, PPF), 80D (medical insurance), 80E (education loan), 80G, HRA, and professional tax.

  Layer 4 — Monthly TDS Computation: Distributes annual tax liability across remaining months. Adjusts each month for changes in salary, regime switches, new declarations, and year-end reconciliation.

  Layer 5 — Form 16 Generation: At year end, generates Form 16 Part A (from TRACES — TDS deposited) and Part B (salary computation, exemptions, deductions, and net taxable income). Provides digital delivery to employees.

TRACES Reconciliation & TDS Credit Management
TDS / TCS COMPLIANCE


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Reconciles TDS deposited, TDS returns filed, and TDS credits reflected on TRACES — ensuring that all deducted and deposited amounts are correctly credited to deductees and all discrepancies are corrected.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — TRACES Data Download: Downloads TDS liability, challan details, and Form 26AS data from the TRACES portal. Extracts the status of all TDS returns filed (processed / defective / corrections pending).

  Layer 2 — Challan-to-Return Matching: Matches each TDS deposit challan against the corresponding entries in the quarterly TDS return. Identifies challan consumption mismatches — challan not consumed, over-consumed, or wrong AY applied.

  Layer 3 — Deductee Credit Verification: Checks whether TDS deducted from deductees is appearing in their Form 26AS. Identifies credits not appearing — due to TDS return errors, PAN mismatches, or unprocessed returns.

  Layer 4 — Correction Return Management: For errors identified, prepares correction statements (C1/C2/C3/C4/C5 corrections) using RPU. Validates the correction before submission to TRACES.

  Layer 5 — Demand Resolution: For TRACES demands (short deduction, short deposit, interest), prepares response — either correction return, payment, or legal response as appropriate.

Use Cases 17-20
DTAA Benefits, CbCR, Equalization Levy & FEMA Compliance


17
DTAA (Double Taxation Avoidance Agreement) Benefit Determination
INTERNATIONAL TAX


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Analyses cross-border payments and determines eligibility for DTAA treaty benefits — reduced withholding tax rates, exemptions, and Permanent Establishment exposure — to optimise international tax costs.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Cross-Border Payment Identification: Reads all outbound payments to non-residents from the treasury and accounts payable system — royalties, technical service fees, dividends, interest, software payments, and FTS charges.

  Layer 2 — Treaty Identification: Identifies the applicable DTAA between India and the recipient's country of residence. Maps the payment type to the relevant treaty article — Article 12 (Royalties), Article 13 (Capital Gains), Article 7 (Business Profits), etc.

  Layer 3 — Tax Residency Certificate Validation: Validates that the non-resident recipient has provided a valid Tax Residency Certificate (TRC) — prerequisite for treaty benefit under Section 90(4). Checks TRC validity period and other mandatory Form 10F information.

  Layer 4 — Beneficial Ownership & LOB: Analyses whether the recipient qualifies as the beneficial owner of the income (required for most treaties). Applies Limitation of Benefits (LOB) clause analysis where the treaty contains one.

  Layer 5 — Withholding Tax Computation: Computes withholding tax at the beneficial treaty rate vs. the domestic TDS rate. Generates the Form 15CA/15CB package for FEMA compliance along with the Treaty benefit claim.

Payment Identification Agent
Cross-border payments identified
Non-resident payments classified by nature
02
Treaty Mapping Agent

Treaty article and rate mapped to payment type
03
TRC Validation Agent
TRC and Form 10F verified
Valid TRC confirmed; benefit eligibility established
04
Beneficial Ownership Agent
Beneficial owner analysis performed
Pass-through / conduit structure risk assessed
05
Withholding Agent
Treaty rate vs. domestic rate compared
Lower of treaty or domestic rate applied
06
Form 15CA/CB Agent
FEMA compliance forms prepared
Form 15CA (CA/self) and 15CB (CA certificate) generated
07
Documentation Agent
Treaty benefit file assembled
TRC, Form 10F, analysis, and working archived

18
Country-by-Country Reporting (CbCR) Compliance
INTERNATIONAL TAX


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Prepares the Country-by-Country Report (CbCR) — Form 3CEAA — for multinational enterprises with consolidated revenue above ₹5,500 Cr, detailing revenue, profit, tax, and employee data by jurisdiction.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Applicability Check: Determines CbCR obligation — is the Indian entity the Ultimate Parent Entity (UPE), surrogate parent, or a constituent entity? Checks the ₹5,500 Cr consolidated revenue threshold and applicable filing jurisdiction.

  Layer 2 — Data Aggregation by Jurisdiction: Aggregates revenue, profit before tax, income tax paid, income tax accrued, stated capital, accumulated earnings, employees, and tangible assets by jurisdiction from consolidated group data.

  Layer 3 — Constituent Entity List: Compiles the complete list of constituent entities — legal name, jurisdiction of tax residence, jurisdiction of incorporation, and principal business activities.

  Layer 4 — Data Validation: Validates CbCR data for internal consistency — revenue aggregation, profit reconciliation against consolidated P&L, and data completeness per jurisdiction.

  Layer 5 — Form 3CEAA Filing: Prepares Form 3CEAA (Table 1 — jurisdiction summary, Table 2 — constituent entity list, Table 3 — additional notes) and files on the income tax portal by the due date (on or before filing of ITR).

19
Equalization Levy Compliance
INTERNATIONAL TAX


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Manages Equalization Levy compliance — on digital advertising services (6%) and e-commerce operator transactions (2%) — computing, depositing, and filing the annual statement of Equalization Levy.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Transaction Identification: Identifies transactions subject to Equalization Levy — payments to non-resident digital advertising platforms (Google, LinkedIn, Meta) and purchases from non-resident e-commerce operators (Amazon marketplace, etc.).

  Layer 2 — Rate Determination: Applies the correct Equalization Levy rate — 6% on specified services (digital advertising) paid to non-residents without PE in India, and 2% on e-commerce operator transactions (where levy applicable).

  Layer 3 — DTAA / Income Tax Interaction: Checks whether the transaction is also subject to income tax (TDS) — where income tax applies, Equalization Levy does not. Ensures no double taxation between EL and TDS on the same transaction.

  Layer 4 — Levy Deposit: Computes the Equalization Levy amount. Generates Challan for quarterly deposit (June, September, December, March). Ensures deposit by the 7th of the month following the quarter.

  Layer 5 — Annual Statement: Prepares the Annual Statement of Equalization Levy (Form 1) — summary of all transactions and levy paid — and files on the income tax portal by June 30.

20
FEMA Compliance for Outbound Payments
INTERNATIONAL TAX


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Ensures all outbound cross-border payments comply with FEMA — correct purpose codes, Form 15CA/15CB filings, RBI reporting, and LRS (Liberalised Remittance Scheme) compliance for applicable transactions.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Payment Classification: Reads each outbound foreign payment. Classifies by FEMA purpose code — current account (trade payments, services, royalties) vs. capital account (investments, loans, equity). Determines applicable regulatory framework.

  Layer 2 — Form 15CA/15CB Determination: Determines which Form 15CA category applies — Part A (payment ≤ ₹5 lakh, non-taxable), Part B (CA certificate), Part C (large payments, CA certificate), or Part D (exempt from tax).

  Layer 3 — CA Certificate Coordination: For Form 15CB (required for Part C payments), prepares the supporting package for the CA — payment details, tax analysis, TRC, DTAA analysis, and TDS computation — to enable efficient CA certification.

  Layer 4 — RBI Reporting: Identifies transactions requiring RBI reporting — ODI (Overseas Direct Investment), FLA (Foreign Liabilities and Assets), and ECB (External Commercial Borrowings) filings. Prepares and files the reports within prescribed timelines.

  Layer 5 — Compliance Audit Trail: Maintains a complete FEMA compliance register for every cross-border payment — purpose code, Form 15CA/15CB reference, bank remittance details, and RBI acknowledgement where applicable.

AGENT FLOW — STEP BY STEP

01
Payment Classification Agent

FEMA purpose code and regulatory framework determined
02
Form 15CA Agent
Correct Form 15CA part determined

21
Customs Duty Classification & Valuation
INDIRECT TAX — OTHERS


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Classifies imported and exported goods under the correct Customs Tariff Heading, determines assessable value, computes customs duty, and identifies applicable exemption notifications — minimising duty costs and avoiding classification disputes.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — HS Code Classification: Reads the product description, technical specification, and import invoice. Applies the Customs Tariff (based on WCO Harmonised System) to assign the correct 8-digit HS code. Validates against DGFT Product Schedule.

  Layer 2 — Duty Rate Determination: Reads the Basic Customs Duty (BCD), IGST, Compensation Cess, and Anti-Dumping Duty rates applicable to the HS code. Applies MFN rate vs. FTA preferential rate where applicable.

  Layer 3 — Assessable Value Computation: Computes customs assessable value per Customs Valuation Rules — CIF value (cost + insurance + freight), additions for royalties/licence fees/commissions, and deductions for post-importation costs.

  Layer 4 — Exemption Notification Identification: Searches the current Customs Notification database for applicable exemptions — project imports (9801), EOU/SEZ benefits, EPCG scheme, advance authorisation, and end-use exemptions.

  Layer 5 — ICEGATE Filing & Bill of Entry: Prepares the Bill of Entry data for ICEGATE e-filing. Computes total duty outgo. Identifies classification risk and prepares advance ruling application if high-value/high-risk classification.

Classification Agent

22
Professional Tax Compliance
INDIRECT TAX — OTHERS


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Manages Professional Tax compliance across all states of operation — registration, monthly/annual deduction from salaries, employer PT payment, and return filing — for all applicable employees and establishments.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Applicability Assessment: Determines Professional Tax applicability per state — different states have different rates, thresholds, and filing frequencies. Checks which states the company has establishments in and the applicable PT Act.

  Layer 2 — Employee Slab Application: For each state, applies the monthly salary slab to determine the PT liability per employee — typically ₹200/month for salaries above the threshold (varies by state).

  Layer 3 — Employer PT Computation: Computes the employer's own PT liability (Profession Tax on the entity) where applicable — different from the employee deduction obligation. Varies by state.

  Layer 4 — Deduction & Deposit: Deducts PT from employee salaries in the correct month (most states: monthly, Karnataka: annual). Initiates deposit to the respective state PT authority within the due date.

  Layer 5 — Return Filing: Files the periodic PT return in each state — monthly (Maharashtra, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu), annual (others) — with the correct enrolment certificate details and payment challan.

Stamp Duty Compliance on Transactions
INDIRECT TAX — OTHERS


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Identifies stamp duty obligations on contracts, agreements, and instruments — computing correct duty per applicable state Act, managing e-stamping/physical stamping, and ensuring adjudication where required.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Instrument Classification: Reads contracts, agreements, and instruments being executed — loan agreements, lease deeds, share transfers, mortgage deeds, partnership deeds, powers of attorney, indemnity bonds. Determines instrument type under applicable Stamp Act.

  Layer 2 — State Act Identification: Determines which state Stamp Act applies — based on where the instrument is executed or where the property is situated. Different states have different stamp duty rates for the same instrument.

  Layer 3 — Duty Computation: Computes stamp duty per the applicable schedule — ad valorem duty on the instrument value / consideration, or a fixed amount, applying any caps or reductions for group transactions.

  Layer 4 — E-Stamping / Physical Stamping: Initiates e-stamping through the SHCIL e-stamp portal for applicable states. For states not yet on e-stamping, identifies the correct physical stamp paper requirement.

  Layer 5 — Adjudication Management: For high-value instruments or ambiguous instruments, manages the adjudication process — filing the instrument with the Collector of Stamps, responding to queries, and obtaining the adjudication order.

24
Tax on Real Estate Transactions
INDIRECT TAX — OTHERS


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Manages all tax compliance for real estate transactions — GST on under-construction property purchases, TDS under Section 194IA on property sales, stamp duty, and income tax implications of property transactions.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Transaction Classification: Reads the real estate transaction — purchase, sale, lease, or development. Determines the nature of transaction — under-construction (GST applicable), ready-to-move (no GST), or commercial lease (GST applicable).

  Layer 2 — GST on Real Estate: For under-construction purchases, computes GST — 1% for affordable housing, 5% for other residential (without ITC), 12% for commercial (with ITC). Validates whether the developer has opted for the old scheme.

  Layer 3 — TDS under Section 194IA: For immovable property purchases exceeding ₹50 lakh, deducts TDS at 1% of the consideration (or stamp duty value, whichever higher). Deposits via Form 26QB and issues Form 16B to seller.

  Layer 4 — Capital Gains Analysis: For property sales, computes capital gains — LTCG (> 2 years holding) at 20% with indexation (pre-July 2024) or 12.5% without indexation (post-July 2024 amendment), STCG at slab rate. Identifies Section 54/54F reinvestment exemptions.

  Layer 5 — Stamp Duty & Registration: Identifies state-specific stamp duty on the property transaction. Flags circle rate vs. consideration comparison — the higher of the two is used for stamp duty and TDS base under Section 50C/43CA.

25
Tax on Employee Benefits & Perquisites
INDIRECT TAX — OTHERS


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Computes the taxable value of all employee perquisites — company car, rent-free accommodation, ESOP, meal vouchers, club memberships, and other benefits — ensuring correct TDS under Section 192.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Perquisite Identification: Reads the company's HR benefits database and expense records. Identifies all perquisites provided — accommodation, car, ESOP, meal coupons, club memberships, gifts, interest-free loans.

  Layer 2 — Taxable Value Computation: Applies Rule 3 of Income Tax Rules to compute the taxable perquisite value — accommodation (15%/10%/7.5% of salary depending on city), car (per month flat rate), and prescribed formula for others.

  Layer 3 — ESOP Perquisite: For ESOPs, computes the perquisite at the time of exercise — difference between FMV of shares on exercise date and exercise price paid. Applies capital gains on subsequent sale.

  Layer 4 — Exempt Perquisites: Identifies perquisites specifically exempt from tax — meal coupons up to ₹50/meal, medical reimbursement (pre-Budget limits), uniform allowance, and laptop/computer provided for business use.

  Layer 5 — Perquisite TDS Integration: Adds the monthly perquisite value to the employee's salary for TDS computation. Ensures perquisites are included in Form 16 Part B and reflected in the employee's ITR.

TAR   Tax Accounting & Reporting
Use Cases 26-29
Deferred Tax, Tax Provision, Risk Register & Tax Data Management


26
Deferred Tax Computation (Ind AS 12 / AS 22)
TAX ACCOUNTING & REPORTING


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Computes deferred tax assets and liabilities arising from temporary differences between book and tax carrying values — ensuring correct DTA/DTL recognition, MAT credit entitlement computation, and adequate disclosure under Ind AS 12.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Temporary Difference Identification: Compares book carrying values and tax base of every asset and liability. Identifies taxable temporary differences (future tax liability — DTL) and deductible temporary differences (future tax benefit — DTA).

  Layer 2 — DTA Recoverability Assessment: For each DTA, assesses recoverability — is it probable that sufficient future taxable profit will be available? Applies the 'probable' criterion conservatively for loss-making entities.

  Layer 3 — Key Timing Differences: Computes deferred tax for the most common items — depreciation differences (book vs. tax), Section 43B disallowances (deductible on payment), provisions (deductible on crystallisation), and ESOP cost (deductible on exercise).

  Layer 4 — MAT Credit Entitlement: Computes MAT credit — where MAT is higher than normal tax, the difference is recognised as DTA (MAT credit entitlement) to be carried forward and set off in years where normal tax exceeds MAT.

  Layer 5 — Balance Sheet Recognition & Disclosure: Computes the movement in DTA/DTL for the period. Prepares the tax expense note — current tax, deferred tax, reconciliation of effective tax rate to statutory rate (ETR reconciliation).

AGENT FLOW — STEP BY STEP

01
Asset-Liability Comparison Agent
Book vs. tax carrying values compared
Temporary differences identified per asset/liability
02
DTA Recoverability Agent
Future taxable profit assessed
DTA recognised or restricted based on recoverability
03
Depreciation Agent
Book vs. tax depreciation compared
Section 32 WDV vs. book WDV temporary difference computed
04
Section 43B Agent
Disallowed provisions and accruals identified
Timing difference from statutory payment conditions
05
MAT Credit Agent
MAT credit entitlement computed
DTA for excess MAT over normal tax recognised
06
Movement Agent
Opening vs. closing DTA/DTL compared
Deferred tax charge or credit for the period computed
07
Disclosure Agent
ETR reconciliation and note prepared
Tax expense note for financial statements assembled

CONFIDENCE-BASED ROUTING

Outcome:  Deferred tax computed accurately every quarter. ETR reconciliation ready for auditors. MAT credit utilisation optimised. Uncertain tax positions properly recognised.


27
Tax Provision & Effective Tax Rate Analysis
TAX ACCOUNTING & REPORTING


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Computes the quarterly and annual tax provision — current tax, deferred tax, and uncertain tax positions — and analyses the effective tax rate with a full book-to-tax reconciliation for CFO and board reporting.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Current Tax Provision: Computes current income tax provision — applying the enacted tax rate to estimated taxable income for the period. Includes surcharge, cess, and MAT where applicable.

  Layer 2 — Deferred Tax Provision: Applies the period's deferred tax movement (from UC26) to compute total tax expense. Validates that deferred tax rate equals the enacted rate expected to apply when timing differences reverse.

  Layer 3 — Uncertain Tax Position (UTP) Reserve: Identifies uncertain tax positions — matters in litigation, aggressive positions taken, untested interpretations. Applies recognition threshold (more-likely-than-not under Ind AS 12 App C) and measures the reserve.

  Layer 4 — Effective Tax Rate (ETR) Computation: Computes the ETR — total tax expense / pre-tax profit. Builds the ETR reconciliation — starting from statutory rate (25.17%), adjusted for permanent differences, exempt income, non-deductible expenses, deductions, and UTP movements.

  Layer 5 — CFO & Board Reporting: Prepares the tax provision summary for quarterly board packs — current provision, deferred tax movement, UTP reserve changes, cash tax payments, and year-to-date ETR vs. budget.

AGENT FLOW — STEP BY STEP

01
Current Tax Agent
YTD taxable income estimated
Current tax provision for period computed
02
Deferred Tax Agent
Deferred tax movement applied
Total tax expense = current + deferred
03
UTP Agent

Reserve for uncertain tax positions computed
04
ETR Agent
Effective tax rate computed
Book rate vs. statutory rate reconciliation prepared
05
Variance Agent

Variance drivers identified and explained
06
Provision Agent

Current tax payable and deferred tax ledger entries prepared
07
Board Report Agent

CFO tax provision commentary prepared

28
Tax Risk Register & Control Framework
TAX ACCOUNTING & REPORTING


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Maintains a live tax risk register — identifying, assessing, and tracking all tax risks across GST, income tax, customs, and international tax — with controls testing and board-level disclosure recommendations.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Tax Risk Identification: Continuously scans for tax risks — new legislation, GST notifications, CBDT circulars, court judgements, pending notices, open assessment years, aggressive positions, and cross-border exposures.

  Layer 2 — Risk Assessment: For each identified risk, computes a risk score — likelihood (1-5) × impact (1-5). Classifies risks as Critical (>15), High (10-15), Medium (5-10), or Low (<5).

  Layer 3 — Control Mapping: Maps existing controls against each risk — reconciliation processes, return review controls, authority matrix for sign-off, and system-generated validations. Identifies control gaps.

  Layer 4 — Action Tracking: Tracks remediation actions — who is responsible, due date, and completion status. Escalates overdue actions to the CFO.

  Layer 5 — Board Disclosure: Prepares the tax risk disclosure for the board / audit committee — material tax uncertainties, contingent liabilities, open litigation exposure, and tax provision adequacy assessment.

Action Agent

Tax Technology & Data Management
TAX ACCOUNTING & REPORTING


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Manages the quality, completeness, and readiness of tax data across ERP and tax systems — ensuring that master data, transaction coding, and system configurations support accurate and timely tax compliance across all tax types.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Tax Master Data Governance: Monitors key tax master data — vendor GSTIN, customer GSTIN, HSN/SAC codes, TDS sections per vendor, exemption categories, and cost centre tax treatment. Flags outdated or missing data.

  Layer 2 — Tax Configuration Validation: Periodically validates ERP tax configuration — GST rate determination logic, TDS rate tables, place of supply rules, reverse charge applicability, and SEZ/export settings — against current law.

  Layer 3 — Transaction Coding Audit: Samples completed transactions and reviews tax coding applied — correct GST rate, correct TDS section, correct place of supply, and correct exemption applied. Identifies systematic miscoding.

  Layer 4 — Data Quality Reporting: Computes data quality metrics — % of vendor records with valid GSTIN, % of invoices with HSN codes, % of payments with correct TDS section. Reports to tax and finance teams.

  Layer 5 — Legislative Change Management: Monitors GST Council decisions, CBDT notifications, and budget amendments. Assesses impact on ERP configuration and compliance processes. Coordinates system updates before effective dates.

TPO   Tax Planning & Optimisation
Use Cases 30-33
Business Structure Planning, Dispute Prevention, Tax Cash Flow & Legislative Impact


30
Tax-Efficient Business Structure Planning
TAX PLANNING & OPTIMISATION


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Analyses the company's legal structure, inter-entity transactions, and operational footprint to identify tax planning opportunities — deductions, holidays, regime changes, and restructuring options — and quantifies the tax saving potential.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Entity Structure Analysis: Maps all legal entities, their tax status (Section 10A/10B/10AA SEZ units, 80-IC hill area units, newly incorporated companies, startups), and the tax holiday periods applicable.

  Layer 2 — Deduction Optimisation: Identifies and maximises all available deductions — 80JJAA (additional employee cost), 80M (dividend income deduction), 35(1)(ii) (scientific research), 32AC/32AD (investment allowance), and 36(1)(ix) for employee welfare.

  Layer 3 — Regime Analysis: Evaluates whether the company should opt for the concessional tax regime (Section 115BAA at 22% + surcharge + cess) vs. the old regime with deductions. Computes break-even point.

  Layer 4 — Loss Utilisation: Tracks brought-forward business losses, unabsorbed depreciation, and capital losses. Plans income recognition timing and business restructuring to maximise loss utilisation within the 8-year carry-forward window.

  Layer 5 — Restructuring Options: Evaluates tax implications of business restructuring — mergers, demergers, slump sales, asset transfers — under Sections 47, 47A, 72A, and the tax neutrality conditions.

31
Tax Dispute Prevention & Advance Rulings
TAX PLANNING & OPTIMISATION


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Proactively identifies high-risk tax positions, structures advance ruling applications, and manages Advance Pricing Agreements (APAs) — preventing disputes before they arise rather than fighting them after.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — High-Risk Position Identification: Scans tax positions for potential dispute triggers — novel transactions, positions contrary to recent judgements, industry-specific CBDT focus areas, and transactions flagged in previous assessments.

  Layer 2 — Advance Ruling Eligibility: Assesses whether an Advance Ruling application to the AAR/AAAR (under GST) or Board for Advance Rulings (under Income Tax) is feasible and beneficial for the identified issue.

  Layer 3 — APA Application: For high-volume transfer pricing transactions, evaluates the feasibility of an Advance Pricing Agreement — bilateral APA with foreign tax authority through CBDT — to provide certainty on TP pricing for 5 years.

  Layer 4 — Ruling Application Preparation: Prepares the advance ruling application — statement of facts, legal questions, taxpayer's view, and supporting analysis. Manages the application process and hearing representation.

  Layer 5 — Ruling Outcome Management: Monitors ruling decisions. Assesses impact on current year and future year tax positions. For adverse rulings, evaluates appeal options or position restructuring.

Tax Cash Flow Planning
TAX PLANNING & OPTIMISATION


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Plans the company's tax cash outflows across the year — advance tax instalments, TDS deposits, GST payments, and custom duty — optimising payment timing within legal limits to improve working capital.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Tax Calendar Assembly: Compiles all tax payment due dates for the year — advance tax instalments (June, September, December, March), monthly GST, monthly TDS deposits, customs duty on imports, and professional tax.

  Layer 2 — Cash Outflow Projection: Projects the tax cash outflow for each period — estimated advance tax (based on current year forecast), recurring TDS deposits (based on AP pipeline), and GST net payable (output minus ITC).

  Layer 3 — Working Capital Impact: Models the working capital impact of tax outflows — GST paid on purchases before input credit recovery, advance tax paid before year-end reconciliation, and TDS deposited before credit recovery.

  Layer 4 — Legal Optimisation: Identifies legal optimisation opportunities — using ITC credit before cash payment for GST, timing of purchases to maximise input credit in the period, and utilisation of existing credit ledger balance.

  Layer 5 — Treasury Integration: Integrates the tax cash flow plan with the treasury cash flow forecast — ensuring sufficient liquidity on all due dates and no surprise demands on cash.

AGENT FLOW — STEP BY STEP

Calendar Agent

Complete tax payment calendar for the year assembled

ITC Agent

33
New Tax Law Impact Assessment
TAX PLANNING & OPTIMISATION


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Monitors Union Budget announcements, Finance Acts, GST Council decisions, CBDT/CBIC circulars, and significant court judgements — and assesses their impact on the company's tax position and compliance obligations within 48 hours.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Legislative Monitoring: Continuously monitors the Ministry of Finance, CBDT, CBIC, GST Council, and SEBI for new legislation, notifications, circulars, and press releases. Also monitors High Court and Supreme Court tax judgements.

  Layer 2 — Relevance Filtering: Filters legislative changes for relevance to the company — based on business sectors, transaction types, entity structure, and tax positions. Avoids alerting for changes that don't apply.

  Layer 3 — Impact Quantification: For relevant changes, quantifies the financial impact — change in tax liability, new compliance obligations, cost of new reporting requirements, and system change requirements.

  Layer 4 — Action Plan: Identifies the actions required — ERP configuration changes, return preparation process changes, new registrations, revised vendor/customer communications, and training needs.

  Layer 5 — CFO Brief: Prepares a concise CFO impact brief within 48 hours of significant announcements — what changed, financial impact, action required, and timeline.

SFR   Statutory Filings & Reporting
Use Cases 34-36
Tax Audit Report (Form 3CD), SFT Filing & Pillar Two Compliance


34
Tax Audit Report (Form 3CD) Preparation
STATUTORY FILINGS


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Prepares the comprehensive tax audit report in Form 3CD — covering all 44 clauses including depreciation, MSME payments, SFT reporting, related party transactions, and TDS compliance — reducing audit time and ensuring completeness.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Clause-Wise Data Extraction: For each of the 44 clauses of Form 3CD, extracts the relevant data from the ERP and accounting records — depreciation schedules, loan and advance details, payments to MSMEs, and TDS deduction data.

  Layer 2 — Depreciation Schedule (Clause 18): Computes the opening WDV, additions, deletions, and closing WDV for each block of assets per Section 32. Reconciles with the fixed asset register and tax computation.

  Layer 3 — MSME Payment Clause (Clause 22): Identifies all payments to registered MSMEs delayed beyond 45 days. Disallows such amounts under Section 43B(h) and provides the schedule for Form 3CD disclosure.

  Layer 4 — TDS Non-Deduction / Short Deduction (Clause 34): Compiles all instances of TDS non-deduction or short-deduction across all vendor payments. Assesses the Section 40(a)(ia) disallowance impact.

  Layer 5 — Auditor Package: Assembles the complete Form 3CD data package for the tax auditor — clause-wise workings, supporting schedules, reconciliations, and explanatory notes — enabling the auditor to efficiently certify and file.

Related Party Agent

06
Data Package Agent

07

Outcome:  Form 3CD preparation from 4 weeks to 1 week. Auditor efficiency improved significantly. MSME payment monitoring and disallowance prevention improved. TDS gaps identified and corrected proactively.


35
Statement of Financial Transactions (SFT) Filing
STATUTORY FILINGS


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Identifies, compiles, and files the Statement of Financial Transactions (Form 61A) for all specified high-value transactions — property purchases, cash deposits, credit card payments, mutual fund transactions, and more.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — SFT Transaction Identification: Scans all transactions for those meeting SFT reporting thresholds — property purchases above ₹30 lakh, cash deposits above ₹10 lakh, credit card payments above ₹1 lakh (cash) / ₹10 lakh (other), foreign exchange above ₹10 lakh.

  Layer 2 — Obligor Identification: Determines which entity is the SFT filer — the company as a buyer/seller of specified assets, or the company's bank/broker on the company's behalf. Ensures no duplication with financial institution SFT.

  Layer 3 — PAN Collection & Validation: For each SFT transaction, ensures the counterparty PAN is collected, validated, and linked. SFT filing with missing or invalid PAN attracts penalty.

  Layer 4 — Form 61A Data Compilation: Aggregates qualifying transactions into Form 61A format — transaction code, PAN of counterparty, amount, and date. Validates the data file against the schema.

  Layer 5 — Filing & Correction: Files Form 61A on the Income Tax Reporting Portal (ITDREIN) by May 31. For corrections, files correction statements. Reconciles filed SFT against AIS data.

AGENT FLOW — STEP BY STEP

Transaction Screen Agent

Qualifying transactions identified

Threshold Agent
Cumulative transaction value tracked per counterparty
Threshold breach and reporting obligation confirmed
03

Valid PAN confirmed for all SFT transactions
04
Data Compilation Agent
Form 61A data structured
Transaction code, PAN, amount, date compiled per transaction
05
Validation Agent

File quality checked before upload
06
Filing Agent

Filing acknowledgement obtained by May 31
07
AIS Reconciliation Agent

Discrepancies corrected through supplementary filing

CONFIDENCE-BASED ROUTING

Applicability assessed; process updated
Tax team update

Outcome:  SFT filed by May 31 every year — zero late filing penalty. All high-value transactions reported. AIS alignment confirmed. Counterparty PAN collection systematic.


36
Pillar Two (Global Minimum Tax) Compliance
STATUTORY FILINGS


WHAT THE AGENT DOES

  Monitors and computes the OECD Pillar Two Global Minimum Tax (GloBE) exposure — assessing top-up tax liability by jurisdiction, GloBE safe harbours eligibility, and preparing for the STTR/domestic minimum tax where applicable.

REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS

  Layer 1 — Applicability Check: Assesses Pillar Two applicability — consolidated group revenue threshold (€750 million), jurisdictions covered, and the company's role (UPE, IIR, UTPR). Tracks India's implementation of the domestic minimum top-up tax (DMTT).

  Layer 2 — GloBE Income Computation: Computes GloBE income by jurisdiction — starting from financial accounting profit, applying GloBE adjustments (excluded dividends, excluded equity gains, specific deductions), and excluding jurisdictions below the de minimis threshold.

  Layer 3 — Covered Tax Computation: Computes covered taxes by jurisdiction — income taxes paid and deferred taxes, adjusted for GloBE timing difference elections and deferred tax recapture.

  Layer 4 — Effective Tax Rate (ETR) by Jurisdiction: Computes the GloBE ETR per jurisdiction — Covered Taxes / GloBE Income. Identifies jurisdictions with ETR below 15% — subject to top-up tax.

  Layer 5 — Safe Harbour Assessment & Top-Up Tax: Evaluates Transitional Safe Harbours (CbCR-based ETR, Substance-Based Income Exclusion) to reduce compliance burden. For non-safe-harbour jurisdictions, computes Qualified Domestic Top-up Tax (QDTT) and IIR top-up tax.

AGENT FLOW — STEP BY STEP

01
Applicability Agent
Group revenue threshold and jurisdictions checked
Pillar Two obligation scope determined
02
GloBE Income Agent
GloBE income by jurisdiction computed
Adjusted accounting profit per GloBE rules
03
Covered Tax Agent
Covered taxes by jurisdiction compiled
Income tax + deferred tax adjustments applied
04
ETR Agent
GloBE ETR per jurisdiction computed
Jurisdictions below 15% identified
05
Safe Harbour Agent
Transitional safe harbours applied
CbCR safe harbour eligibility assessed per jurisdiction
06
Top-Up Tax Agent
Top-up tax computed for non-safe-harbour jurisdictions
QDTT and IIR liability calculated
07
GloBE Return Agent
GloBE information return prepared
Form for filing in UPE jurisdiction assembled

CONFIDENCE-BASED ROUTING

Confidence
Action
Human Effort

Compliance documentation; no additional tax
Group tax team review

CFO + group tax decision

India DMTT compliance procedures followed
Tax + legal team
New Pillar Two jurisdiction
Country-specific rules assessed; compliance calendar updated
Group tax team

Outcome:  Pillar Two exposure computed by jurisdiction. Safe harbour eligibility maximised. Top-up tax liability quantified for CFO. Zero non-compliance with Global Minimum Tax rules.

SR Strategic Sourcing & Supplier Discovery

Spend Analysis, Supplier Discovery, RFx Management, Bid Evaluation, Auctions, Negotiations, Supplier Risk, TCO Analysis, Category Strategy, Market Intelligence

01
Spend Analysis & Category Intelligence
STRATEGIC SOURCING
WHAT THE AGENT DOES
Analyzes organizational spend across categories, suppliers, business units, and geographies to identify sourcing opportunities, consolidation potential, maverick spending, and cost savings targets using AI-powered spend classification and analytics.
REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS
Layer 1 — Spend Data Aggregation & Cleansing: Aggregates spend data from multiple sources including AP systems, procurement systems, corporate cards, and P-Cards. Cleanses vendor names using fuzzy matching and entity resolution. Normalizes transaction data across different systems and formats. Handles multi-currency spend conversion.
Layer 2 — AI-Powered Spend Classification: Classifies spend into standardized taxonomy (UNSPSC, custom categories) using machine learning trained on historical classifications. Automatically categorizes unclassified spend with confidence scores. Handles multi-classification for complex items. Continuously learns from user corrections.
Layer 3 — Supplier Consolidation Analysis: Identifies suppliers providing similar goods/services across the organization. Detects duplicate suppliers with different names or entity structures. Calculates consolidation opportunity value. Analyzes tail spend concentration and rationalization potential.
Layer 4 — Maverick Spend Detection: Identifies off-contract purchases and unauthorized suppliers. Compares actual purchasing patterns against negotiated contracts. Flags purchases bypassing preferred suppliers. Quantifies contract leakage and maverick spend volume. Analyzes root causes of non-compliance.
Layer 5 — Savings Opportunity Identification: Identifies cost savings opportunities including volume consolidation, supplier rationalization, contract renegotiation, payment term optimization, and alternative sourcing. Quantifies potential savings by category and opportunity type. Prioritizes opportunities by impact and feasibility.
AGENT FLOW — STEP BY STEP

02
Supplier Discovery & Market Intelligence
STRATEGIC SOURCING
WHAT THE AGENT DOES
Discovers and evaluates potential suppliers through market research, supplier databases, industry networks, and AI-powered supplier matching, providing comprehensive supplier intelligence including capabilities, certifications, financial health, and competitive positioning.
REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS
Layer 1 — Multi-Source Supplier Database: Accesses multiple supplier databases (D&B, ThomasNet, industry-specific databases, public registries). Aggregates supplier information including company profiles, capabilities, certifications, customer references, and financial data. Maintains proprietary supplier database with enriched information.
Layer 2 — Capability-Based Supplier Matching: Matches sourcing requirements to supplier capabilities using semantic search and AI matching algorithms. Analyzes supplier product/service offerings, manufacturing capabilities, geographic coverage, capacity, and specializations. Ranks suppliers by fit score.
Layer 3 — Diversity & Certification Verification: Identifies diverse suppliers (minority-owned, women-owned, veteran-owned, small business). Verifies diversity certifications through official certification bodies. Tracks sustainability certifications (ISO 14001, B-Corp), quality certifications (ISO 9001), and industry-specific certifications.
Layer 4 — Financial Health Assessment: Evaluates supplier financial stability using credit ratings, financial statements, payment behavior, and predictive bankruptcy models. Assesses financial risk scores. Identifies financially distressed suppliers requiring monitoring or alternative sourcing.
Layer 5 — Competitive Intelligence Gathering: Analyzes supplier market positioning, competitive landscape, pricing trends, and innovation capabilities. Monitors supplier news, product launches, acquisitions, and strategic initiatives. Provides market intelligence for negotiation leverage.

03
RFx Creation & Management (RFI/RFP/RFQ)
STRATEGIC SOURCING
WHAT THE AGENT DOES
Automates creation, distribution, and management of Request for Information (RFI), Request for Proposal (RFP), and Request for Quotation (RFQ) documents with AI-powered requirement extraction, template management, supplier communication, and response collection.
REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS
Layer 1 — RFx Template Library & Automation: Maintains library of RFx templates by category, spend type, and complexity. Auto-generates RFx documents from templates with AI-populated requirements. Includes standard sections (scope, terms, pricing, qualifications). Customizes templates based on project-specific needs.
Layer 2 — Requirement Extraction & Specification: Extracts requirements from requisitions, specifications, and stakeholder inputs using NLP. Converts unstructured requirements into structured RFx sections. Identifies must-have vs. nice-to-have requirements. Generates technical specification tables and scoring criteria.
Layer 3 — Supplier Selection & Invitation: Recommends suppliers for RFx based on capability matching, past performance, and category expertise. Manages supplier eligibility and qualification criteria. Sends RFx invitations with customized messaging. Tracks invitation acceptance and supplier opt-ins.
Layer 4 — Clarification Management: Manages supplier questions and clarification requests. Consolidates similar questions. Provides responses to all suppliers simultaneously to maintain fairness. Maintains Q&A log. Issues amendments to RFx based on clarifications.
Layer 5 — Response Collection & Organization: Provides supplier portal for response submission. Validates response completeness against required sections. Organizes responses for comparative evaluation. Handles late submissions and extensions. Maintains confidentiality and prevents early disclosure.

04
Bid Evaluation & Scoring Automation
STRATEGIC SOURCING
WHAT THE AGENT DOES
Automates bid evaluation using configurable scoring models, performs quantitative and qualitative analysis, conducts should-cost modeling, identifies best value suppliers, and generates recommendation reports with AI-assisted comparative analysis.
REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS
Layer 1 — Multi-Criteria Scoring Model: Defines weighted scoring criteria including price (cost, TCO), quality (certifications, process capability), delivery (lead time, on-time delivery), service (responsiveness, support), innovation, and sustainability. Allows customization by category. Supports mandatory pass/fail criteria.
Layer 2 — Quantitative Analysis Automation: Automatically extracts and normalizes pricing from supplier responses. Calculates total cost of ownership including shipping, duties, payment terms discount, warranty costs, and lifecycle costs. Performs price-per-unit normalization for comparison.
Layer 3 — Qualitative Scoring with AI: Uses AI to evaluate qualitative responses including technical approach, project methodology, risk mitigation, and innovation. Scores narrative responses based on completeness, relevance, and quality. Identifies strengths and concerns in each response.
Layer 4 — Should-Cost Modeling: Performs should-cost analysis estimating fair market price based on material costs, labor costs, overhead, and reasonable margin. Compares supplier bids to should-cost baseline. Identifies bids significantly above or below should-cost requiring investigation.
Layer 5 — Scenario Analysis & Sensitivity: Performs what-if scenarios with different scoring weights, price changes, or requirement modifications. Conducts sensitivity analysis showing impact of weight changes on supplier ranking. Identifies split award opportunities and multi-sourcing scenarios.

05
E-Auction & Reverse Auction Management
STRATEGIC SOURCING
WHAT THE AGENT DOES
Manages electronic auctions and reverse auctions enabling competitive bidding, real-time price discovery, dynamic lot management, proxy bidding, and market-based pricing optimization for maximum cost savings.
REASONING LOGIC — LAYERS
Layer 1 — Auction Strategy & Design: Determines optimal auction format (reverse, forward, Dutch, Japanese) based on category and market conditions. Defines lot structure (single lot, multiple lots, combinatorial). Sets auction parameters including duration, bid decrements, extensions, and reserve prices.
Layer 2 — Supplier Qualification & Training: Pre-qualifies suppliers for auction participation based on capability and commercial criteria. Provides auction platform training and practice sessions. Issues auction credentials and access. Validates supplier technical readiness.
Layer 3 — Real-Time Auction Execution: Manages live auction event with real-time bid submission and ranking display. Implements automatic bid extensions to prevent last-second sniping. Handles proxy/maximum bidding. Monitors auction integrity and prevents manipulation. Provides real-time analytics during auction.
Layer 4 — Lot Optimization & Bundling: Optimizes lot bundling based on bidder capabilities and prices. Handles split awards across multiple suppliers. Evaluates combinatorial bids for lot combinations. Maximizes savings through optimal lot allocation.
Layer 5 — Post-Auction Award & Negotiation: Performs post-auction analysis comparing results to pre-auction estimates. Manages award decisions and notifications. Handles post-auction negotiations for minor clarifications. Generates savings reports and executive summaries.
